engine.name: GameEngine
engine.stageCompletion: 100% for the current core-first-mvp scope; not a commercial-engine completion claim.
stageClosureNotes: MVP closure is validated through the source-tree default build/test/agent contract plus documented optional desktop runtime and package lanes.
Apple/iOS/Metal validation remains host-gated and is not counted as complete without macOS/Xcode or CI evidence.
Generated-game, sample-game, desktop runtime, and desktop package readiness are limited to the validation recipes listed in this manifest.

languagePolicy.msvcCompileThroughput: MSVC targets use bounded compiler-level parallelism through MK_MSVC_MULTIPROCESSOR_COMPILE_PROCESSES default 2, emitting /MP2 plus /Zf from MK_apply_common_target_options while CMake/MSBuild owns target-level parallelism.
languagePolicy.msvcCompilePdbIsolation: MSVC compiler PDB files are isolated per target through MK_apply_common_target_options COMPILE_PDB_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY and COMPILE_PDB_NAME; supported tools/*.ps1 CMake build entrypoints serialize overlapping CMake build invocations and clear stale Visual Studio .tlog directories with older aliased .lastbuildstate roots without adding /FS.
languagePolicy.msvcIncrementalLinking: Disabled for linkable targets through MK_apply_common_target_options (/INCREMENTAL:NO) so validation builds do not depend on .ilk state.
languagePolicy.modules: Use CMake FILE_SET CXX_MODULES for project modules.
languagePolicy.importStd: Allowed only when CMake reports C++23 standard-library module support for the active generator/toolchain.

commands.removeMergedWorktree: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/remove-merged-worktree.ps1 -WorktreePath <path> [-BaseRef origin/main] [-BaseBranch main] [-Remote origin] [-LocalCheckoutPath <path>] [-DeleteLocalBranch] [-DeleteRemoteBranch]

windowsDiagnosticsToolchain.policy: Official Microsoft host diagnostics only. These tools are not default build dependencies, are not installed by CMake configure, and are blockers only for tasks that explicitly need native debugging, D3D12 debug-layer validation, GPU capture, or ETW/performance evidence.

debuggingToolsForWindows.purpose: Native crash, dump, cdb, and windbg debugging for the Windows/MSVC lane.

graphicsTools.purpose: D3D12 debug layer support for Windows backend diagnostics.

pixOnWindows.purpose: D3D12 GPU captures, timing, and counter investigations.

windowsPerformanceToolkit.purpose: ETW tracing plus CPU and system performance investigations.

windowsDiagnosticsToolchain.hostGatePolicy: Missing Debugging Tools for Windows, Windows Graphics Tools, PIX on Windows, or Windows Performance Toolkit must be reported as a host diagnostics blocker only for the relevant diagnostic task; do not report it as an engine compile, CMake, or default validation failure.

modules.name: MK_assets

Also provides GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry source asset registry validation, deterministic text IO, Asset Identity projection, and texture/mesh/audio/material/scene import metadata projection without importer execution, cooked artifact writes, .geindex package updates, renderer/RHI residency, or package streaming. Also provides asset dependency graph, hot reload snapshot/event tracking, debounce-based recook request scheduling with dependency invalidation, failed-reload rollback state, runtime asset replacement records and ui_atlas_texture dependency edges, canonical cooked GameEngine.UiAtlas metadata validation/serialization through UiAtlasMetadataDocument and UiAtlasMetadataGlyph without source image decoding, real font loading/rasterization, text shaping, renderer texture upload, or production atlas packing, deterministic cooked GameEngine.Tilemap metadata validation/serialization through TilemapMetadataDocument with AssetKind::tilemap and tilemap_texture dependency rows while keeping source decoding, production atlas packing, and native GPU sprite batching unsupported, shader source metadata, generated shader artifact tracking, shader descriptor reflection rows, shader compiler command planning including DXC Vulkan SPIR-V commands with -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3, shell-free shader tool runner validation, compiler output capture contracts, shader include dependency discovery, shader include dependency graph edge generation, deterministic shader artifact manifest serialization including reflection rows, texture/mesh/audio/material/scene import metadata, scene mesh/material/sprite dependency edge kinds, first-party texture/mesh/audio source document decoding including optional deterministic hex byte payloads, GameEngine.MorphMeshCpuSource morph mesh CPU source document serialization and validation with paired GameEngine.CookedMorphMeshCpu cook field emission, GameEngine.AnimationFloatClipSource animation float clip source document serialization and validation with paired GameEngine.CookedAnimationFloatClip cook field emission through the asset import tool, GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource 3D TRS quaternion clip source document serialization and validation with paired GameEngine.CookedAnimationQuaternionClip cook field emission, AssetKind::animation_quaternion_clip, and animation_quaternion_clip import metadata planning, GameEngine.AnimationTransformBindingSource source document validation and deterministic text IO through AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument, serialize_animation_transform_binding_source_document, and deserialize_animation_transform_binding_source_document for curve-target to named transform component authoring without scene-node resolution or runtime payload cooking, deterministic asset import planning, material definition serialization, GameEngine.MaterialGraph graph-authoring IR with deterministic text IO, validation diagnostics, and lowering to MaterialDefinition for the minimal v1 node set (graph_output, constant factors, texture nodes) without shader graph generation, GameEngine.MaterialGraphShaderExport bridge text IO plus deterministic HLSL stub emission for reviewed DXC planning without automatic package cook of export documents, material instance serialization/composition, fixed material factor uniform sizing, explicit backend-neutral material binding metadata with uniform-buffer, sampled-texture, and sampler rows, and Asset Identity is implemented foundation-only, and Source Asset Registry does not complete scene/render/UI/gameplay reference cleanup, renderer/RHI residency, package streaming, or 2D/3D playable vertical slices. External PNG decoded RGBA8 payloads, glTF position-only and interleaved position+normal+uv+tangent mesh payload conversion, common-audio decoded PCM payloads, and first-party GameEngine.Scene source validation/canonicalization are owned by MK_tools adapters so runtime/game code remains cooked-artifact-only.

clip/state/trigger/blend animation state machine, weighted blend tree samples, 1D blend graph samples, layered animation samples with additive flags, authored timeline event-track flattening, timeline event playback, retargeting policy evaluation, Skeleton Pose Foundation v0 with validated skeleton hierarchies, rest poses, local joint transforms using translation, positive scale, and Z-rotation-only radians, joint-track bindings, deterministic pose diagnostics, local pose sampling with missing tracks falling back to rest pose for gameplay, scene, and editor tests, Animation Quaternion Local Pose Foundation v1 with AnimationJointLocalTransform3d, AnimationSkeleton3dDesc, AnimationPose3d, AnimationModelPose3d, validate_animation_skeleton_3d_desc, validate_animation_pose_3d, make_animation_rest_pose_3d, and build_animation_model_pose_3d for unit-quaternion local rotation storage and model-pose matrices, Animation Quaternion Clip Sampling Application v1 with QuatKeyframe, QuatAnimationTrack, is_valid_quat_keyframes, sample_quat_keyframes, AnimationJointTrack3dDesc, validate_animation_joint_tracks_3d, sample_animation_local_pose_3d, AnimationJointTrack3dByteSource, and make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes for normalized shortest-path quaternion keyframe sampling, cooked byte-row decoding, and selected 3D local-pose track application, Animation Skin Payload Foundation v0 with skin joint palette entries, per-vertex influence rows, deterministic skin diagnostics, influence normalization, model-space joint matrices, and skinning palette construction, Animation CPU Skinning Foundation v0 with AnimationCpuSkinningDesc validation and skin_animation_vertices_cpu for deterministic skinned-position output from bind positions, skin influence rows, and palette matrices, Animation CPU Skinned Normal Stream v1 with optional bind_normals validation plus deterministic skinned-normal output, Animation CPU Skinned Tangent Stream v1 with optional bind_tangents validation plus deterministic skinned-tangent output, Animation CPU Skinned Bitangent Handedness v1 with optional bind_tangent_handedness validation plus deterministic skinned-bitangent reconstruction from skinned normal/tangent rows, AnimationMorphMeshCpuDesc and AnimationMorphTargetCpuDesc with validate_animation_morph_mesh_cpu_desc, apply_animation_morph_targets_positions_cpu, apply_animation_morph_targets_normals_cpu, and apply_animation_morph_targets_tangents_cpu for glTF-style additive POSITION, NORMAL, and/or TANGENT (xyz) morph targets, AnimationMorphWeightsTrackDesc with validate_animation_morph_weights_track_desc and sample_animation_morph_weights_at_time for LINEAR mesh weight channels with per-target weights in [0,1], planar two-bone IK through AnimationTwoBoneIkXyBendSide, AnimationTwoBoneIkXyDesc::pole_vector_xy, and solve_animation_two_bone_ik_xy_plane with explicit bend-side plus finite non-collinear pole-vector branch selection, analytic two-bone 3D orientation basis output through AnimationTwoBoneIk3dDesc, AnimationTwoBoneIk3dJointFrame, AnimationTwoBoneIk3dSolution, and solve_animation_two_bone_ik_3d_orientation, deterministic multi-segment XY FABRIK chain IK through AnimationFabrikIkXyBendSide, AnimationFabrikIkXyDesc::bend_side, AnimationFabrikIkXyDesc::pole_vector_xy, AnimationFabrikIkXySegmentRotationLimit, AnimationFabrikIkXySolution, solve_animation_fabrik_ik_xy_chain, AnimationIkLocalRotationLimit, and Backward accumulation, negative-time wrapping, inferred clip duration, tangent-space material/render integration beyond the cooked tangent-frame schema, broader IK constraints beyond the implemented planar segment local Z and 3D swing/twist local clamps, broader generated-game animation/morph integration, async compute scheduling, graphics morph+skin composition, Metal compute morph parity, broad GPU skinning backend parity beyond the recorded D3D12/Vulkan package evidence, and animation graph authoring remain follow-up systems.

modules.name: MK_ai
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only gameplay decision-flow foundation. Behavior Tree Foundation v0 provides value-type BehaviorTreeDesc, BehaviorTreeNodeDesc, BehaviorTreeLeafResult, BehaviorTreeTickResult, diagnostics, and visited-node traces for deterministic memoryless sequence, selector, action, and condition evaluation. Behavior Tree Blackboard Conditions v0 adds typed BehaviorTreeBlackboardValue rows for bool, signed integer, finite double, and string facts, deterministic BehaviorTreeBlackboard snapshots, BehaviorTreeBlackboardCondition bindings, and BehaviorTreeEvaluationContext so condition nodes can read caller-supplied blackboard snapshots while action nodes still use game-supplied leaf statuses. AI Perception Services v1 adds AiPerceptionAgent2D, AiPerceptionTarget2D, AiPerceptionSnapshot2D, build_ai_perception_snapshot_2d, write_ai_perception_blackboard, AiPerceptionReadinessConfig, AiPerceptionReadinessReport, and evaluate_ai_perception_readiness_2d for explicit caller-supplied 2D sight/hearing classification, stable primary-target selection, duplicate target rejection, finite validation, blackboard projection into caller-selected fact keys, and readiness summaries over stable-primary, blackboard-projection, visible/audible-target, and target-budget evidence. AI Behavior Authoring Foundation v1 adds BehaviorAuthoringDocument, BehaviorAuthoringBehaviorDesc, BehaviorAuthoringActionBinding, BehaviorAuthoringValidationContext, BehaviorAuthoringValidationResult, BehaviorAuthoringTraceRow, BehaviorAuthoringDiagnostic, and validate_behavior_authoring_document for value-only generated-game behavior document validation against available blackboard keys and supported game-owned action ids before runtime execution. It reports missing-root, duplicate-node-id, missing-child, empty-composite, leaf-has-children, missing-leaf-result, invalid-leaf-result, duplicate-leaf-result, duplicate-blackboard-key, invalid-blackboard-key, invalid-blackboard-value, duplicate-blackboard-condition, invalid-blackboard-condition, missing-blackboard-key, blackboard-type-mismatch, invalid-blackboard-operator, invalid-agent, invalid-target, duplicate-target-id, invalid-perception-blackboard-key, perception-readiness gaps, calculation-overflow, cycle, max-depth, empty-behavior-id, duplicate-behavior-id, behavior-authoring-invalid-node-id, behavior-authoring-cycle-detected, duplicate-action-binding, missing-action-binding, and unsupported-action diagnostics without owning memory decay, teams/factions, broad event stimuli, AI performance services, async tasks, decorators/services, scene state, physics queries, navigation requests, persistent blackboard services, editor graph authoring, platform APIs, middleware, scripting, or third-party dependencies.

modules.name: MK_audio
modules.purpose: Device-independent audio foundations. Currently provides an AudioMixer that validates audio buses, voices, spatial listener/source descriptors, clip metadata, device render requests, resampling quality, and interleaved float clip sample data; tracks logical clip asset ids; produces deterministic mix commands; builds spatial voice/listener metadata for distance attenuation and stereo pan; plans sample-frame voice scheduling; records streaming buffer availability, device format conversion intent, and underrun diagnostics; exposes AudioGameplayMixRequest, AudioGameplayMixPlan, and plan_gameplay_audio_mix so generated games can fail closed while converting game-owned cue, bus, fade, pause, loop, trigger, and optional spatial metadata rows into mixer-ready bus and voice commands; provides AudioStreamingQueue for decoded chunk append/consume/starvation state without codecs; renders deterministic interleaved float PCM buffers with gain, selectable nearest/linear resampling, channel conversion, and clamping; exposes AudioDeviceStreamRequest, AudioDeviceStreamPlan, plan_audio_device_stream, and render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float so host adapters can fill output queues from AudioMixer PCM using bounded render chunks; and does not open OS audio devices.

modules.name: MK_audio_sdl3
modules.purpose: Optional SDL3 desktop audio adapter. Currently provides SdlAudioRuntime for SDL_INIT_AUDIO lifetime and SdlAudioDevice for default playback streams through SDL_OpenAudioDeviceStream, pause/resume/clear controls, queued-frame reporting, silence queuing, and interleaved float PCM queuing while keeping SDL stream and device handles private.

modules.name: MK_math
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only 2D/3D math primitives for games and engine systems, including vectors, matrices, unit quaternions, quaternion vector rotation, Mat4::rotation_quat, and transforms with position, scale, and radians-based rotation.

modules.name: MK_core
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only deterministic engine core. Currently provides GameApp and headless runner lifecycle contracts, logging contracts, fixed timestep behavior, entity ids, component registry, version data, and dependency-free diagnostics/counter/profile capture plus Trace Event JSON subset export through DiagnosticEvent, CounterSample, ProfileSample, DiagnosticCapture, DiagnosticSummary, ManualProfileClock, SteadyProfileClock, DiagnosticsRecorder, ScopedProfileZone, summarize_diagnostics, DiagnosticsTraceExportOptions, export_diagnostics_trace_json, DiagnosticsOpsHostStatus, DiagnosticsOpsPlanOptions, DiagnosticsOpsArtifact, DiagnosticsOpsPlan, and build_diagnostics_ops_plan without exposing runtime, renderer, platform, editor, SDL3, OS, GPU, network upload, or Dear ImGui APIs.

modules.name: MK_ui_renderer
modules.purpose: First-party runtime UI renderer adapter that consumes MK_ui RendererSubmission payloads through the existing MK_renderer public API without making MK_ui depend on renderer code. Currently provides UiRendererTheme token-to-color mapping, resolves styled UI boxes to renderer colors, converts RendererBox bounds into SpriteCommand rectangles, resolves deterministic MonospaceTextLayoutPolicy glyph rows through UiRendererGlyphAtlasPalette and UiRendererGlyphAtlasBinding by font_family plus glyph id into atlas-backed text glyph sprite submissions, resolves ImageAdapterPayload rows through UiRendererImagePalette and UiRendererImageBinding into image sprite submissions with deterministic resource_id-first and asset_uri-fallback lookup, can attach first-party cooked atlas page and UV metadata for glyph and image bindings, can build UiRendererImagePalette rows from MK_runtime RuntimeUiAtlasPayload package metadata for the native-ui-atlas-package-metadata proof, can build glyph palette rows from RuntimeUiAtlasGlyph package metadata through build_ui_renderer_glyph_atlas_palette_from_runtime_ui_atlas for the Runtime UI Glyph Atlas Package Bridge v1 proof, submits styled UI boxes, resolved text glyphs, and resolved image placeholders through IRenderer, reports text run, text adapter row, text glyph availability/resolution/missing/submission, image placeholder, image sprite submission, missing image resource, accessibility node, and adapter diagnostic availability, and leaves concrete text shaping/font loading/rasterization/glyph atlas generation/source image decoding/production atlas packing/renderer texture upload/OS accessibility bridges behind their separate MK_ui adapter boundaries.

modules.name: MK_platform
modules.purpose: Platform-facing contracts. Currently provides VirtualInput for keyboard-style state, VirtualPointerInput for mouse/touch/pen-style pointer state, VirtualGamepadInput for gamepad buttons and axes, DisplayInfo and WindowDisplayState for monitor/DPI/safe-area metadata plus deterministic monitor selection and window placement policy without native handles, mobile lifecycle/orientation/safe-area/touch/storage/permission contracts for Android and Apple adapters, IClipboard and MemoryClipboard for text clipboard workflows, FileDialogRequest/IFileDialogService/MemoryFileDialogService for asynchronous native-style open/save/folder dialog workflows, CursorMode/ICursor/MemoryCursor for cursor visibility, confinement, and relative-input state, VirtualLifecycle for quit, termination, low-memory, background, and foreground lifecycle events, HeadlessWindow, MemoryFileSystem, RootedFileSystem, file watch backend selection with native backend family reporting, deterministic PollingFileWatcher file event scanning, WindowsFileWatcher backed by Win32 API ReadDirectoryChangesW event scanning behind PIMPL, LinuxFileWatcher backed by inotify event scanning, MacOSFileWatcher backed by FSEvents event scanning, host-unavailable native watcher diagnostics, shell-free ProcessCommand validation, a recording process runner, and a Windows CreateProcessW runner that captures stdout, stderr, exit code, and launch diagnostics without exposing native handles.

modules.purpose: Optional ENet loopback transport adapter built only when MK_ENABLE_NETWORK_ENET=ON and the vcpkg network-enet feature has been bootstrapped. It exposes make_enet_network_transport_adapter through the first-party MK_runtime network_transport facade, keeps ENet headers, ENet* symbols, enet_* functions, socket handles, and native transport handles private to implementation sources, follows ENet initialize/deinitialize, host create/destroy, connect/service, peer send, host flush, and packet destroy lifecycle rules, supports one local loopback client peer with reliable and unreliable packet rows, and stays outside generated-game default recipes, external network readiness, encryption/authentication, matchmaking, NAT traversal, replication, rollback, and broad multiplayer readiness.

modules.name: MK_runtime_scene_rhi
modules.purpose: Backend-neutral bridge from cooked runtime scene packages to renderer-visible scene GPU binding palettes. Currently builds retained SceneGpuBindingPalette resources from RuntimeAssetPackage plus SceneRenderPacket, uploads unique referenced mesh, skinned mesh, texture, and material payloads through MK_runtime_rhi, can explicitly select cooked morph_mesh_cpu package rows through RuntimeSceneGpuBindingOptions::morph_mesh_assets and retain them as SceneMorphGpuBindingPalette resources with a shared morph descriptor set layout, preserves derived mesh layout metadata such as position-only stride 12 and lit tangent-space stride 48 in renderer-visible MeshGpuBinding values, retains mesh uploads, skinned mesh uploads, morph mesh uploads, texture uploads, material descriptor bindings, generated material pipeline layouts, and actual submit-order RuntimeSceneGpuBindingResult::submitted_upload_fences rows in the result, exposes execute_runtime_scene_gpu_upload and RuntimeSceneGpuUploadExecutionReport for host-owned upload execution status with backend-neutral mesh/skinned/morph/texture/material counts, uploaded byte totals, submitted_upload_fence_count, and last_submitted_upload_fence rows derived from that ordered fence list, exposes execute_runtime_scene_gpu_binding_safe_point_teardown for host-owned safe-point GPU teardown handoff with deterministic null-device handle invalidation plus explicit host-native destroy guidance for non-null IRhiDevice implementations without exposing native handles to gameplay, uses one scene-shared material pipeline layout, supports caller-owned material descriptor set layouts while creating a matching pipeline layout itself, rejects incompatible default material descriptor layouts for the current single-pipeline RhiFrameRenderer path, reports deterministic per-asset failures, and keeps MK_runtime, MK_scene, SDL3, native window handles, concrete RHI backends, Dear ImGui, and editor APIs out of generated gameplay code.

modules.name: MK_runtime_scene
modules.purpose: Renderer-free cooked runtime scene gameplay bridge. Runtime Scene Gameplay Instance v0 provides mirakana::runtime_scene::instantiate_runtime_scene, RuntimeSceneInstance, RuntimeSceneReference, RuntimeSceneDiagnostic, RuntimeSceneLoadOptions, RuntimeSceneLoadResult, and find_runtime_scene_nodes_by_name so generated games and engine tests can instantiate a cooked RuntimeAssetPackage scene payload into mirakana::Scene state, enumerate mesh/material/sprite references, validate missing or wrong-kind referenced assets, optionally report duplicate node names, and receive deterministic diagnostics for missing scene assets, wrong scene asset kind, malformed scene payloads, and deserialization failures. Runtime Scene Animation Transform Binding v1 adds resolve_runtime_scene_animation_transform_bindings and apply_runtime_scene_animation_transform_samples to resolve GameEngine.AnimationTransformBindingSource rows against RuntimeSceneInstance node names and apply caller-provided FloatAnimationCurveSample rows to scene node transforms through MK_animation validation, rejecting invalid binding documents, missing node names, and duplicate node names before mutation. Runtime Scene Quaternion Animation Transform Binding v1 adds apply_runtime_scene_animation_pose_3d to resolve AnimationSkeleton3dDesc joint names against RuntimeSceneInstance node names and apply validated AnimationPose3d translation, unit-quaternion rotation converted into the current Transform3D Euler storage, and scale rows before mutation. It does not sample curves from time, add animation graphs, add package formats, add skeletal renderer deformation, expand scalar binding rows beyond translation xyz / rotation z / scale xyz, or depend on MK_renderer, MK_rhi, MK_scene_renderer, editor, platform windows, SDL3, Dear ImGui, or native handles.

modules.name: MK_physics
modules.purpose: Deterministic gameplay physics foundations. Currently provides PhysicsWorld2D and PhysicsWorld3D with validated body descriptions, force accumulation, gravity, damping, fixed-step-friendly integration, deterministic 2D/3D sweep-and-prune broadphase pairs, 2D AABB/circle contacts, 3D AABB/sphere/capsule contacts, collision layer/mask filtering, PhysicsCollisionQueryBatchStatus/PhysicsCollisionQueryBatchDiagnostic/PhysicsCollisionQueryRowStatus/PhysicsCollisionQueryRowDiagnostic plus PhysicsRaycastBatch2DDesc, PhysicsRaycastBatch3DDesc, PhysicsShapeSweepBatch2DDesc, PhysicsShapeSweepBatch3DDesc, PhysicsWorld2D::raycast_batch, PhysicsWorld3D::raycast_batch, PhysicsWorld2D::shape_sweep_batch, and PhysicsWorld3D::shape_sweep_batch for source-indexed value-only query rows with hit/no-hit/invalid-request diagnostics, default-unbounded query counts, explicit positive max_queries budgets, and existing ray/sweep filter semantics, deterministic collision-bounds raycasts with nearest-hit selection and ray mask filtering, trigger body flags plus non-resolving trigger overlap reporting, conservative bounds-based PhysicsShapeSweep2DDesc and PhysicsShapeSweep3DDesc shape sweeps with nearest-hit, ignored-body, trigger-inclusion, mask, and initial-overlap handling, deterministic exact 3D primitive sweeps through PhysicsShape3DDesc::aabb, PhysicsShape3DDesc::sphere, PhysicsShape3DDesc::capsule, PhysicsQueryFilter3D, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DDesc, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DHit, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DResult, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DStatus, PhysicsExactShapeSweep3DDiagnostic, and PhysicsWorld3D::exact_shape_sweep against current AABB/sphere/capsule target primitives with nearest-hit, deterministic epsilon tie-breaking, initial-overlap, disabled-body, ignored-body, trigger-inclusion, mask, conservative false-positive rejection, and invalid-request diagnostics, plus the first-class PhysicsExactSphereCast3DDesc, PhysicsExactSphereCast3DHit, PhysicsExactSphereCast3DResult, PhysicsExactSphereCast3DStatus, PhysicsExactSphereCast3DDiagnostic, and PhysicsWorld3D::exact_sphere_cast convenience wrapper. deterministic contact manifolds through PhysicsContactPoint3D, PhysicsContactManifold3D, and PhysicsWorld3D::contact_manifolds with stable feature ids, warm-start-safe point rows, trigger/disabled exclusion, and solver consumption for current AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule contacts. PhysicsContinuousStep3DConfig, PhysicsContinuousStep3DRow, PhysicsContinuousStep3DResult, and PhysicsWorld3D::step_continuous expose opt-in deterministic 3D CCD rows for fast dynamic AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule bodies swept against current non-trigger AABB/sphere/capsule targets while PhysicsWorld3D::step remains discrete. PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DDesc, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DRowKind, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DRow, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DResult, and evaluate_physics_character_dynamic_policy_3d expose pure non-mutating generated-game push, step-up, slope, grounded, trigger, reciprocal layer/mask, and dynamic-body policy rows over the current query/contact/CCD surface. PhysicsAdvancedController3DDesc, PhysicsMovingPlatform3DDesc, PhysicsAdvancedController3DResult, and plan_physics_advanced_controller_3d expose value-only generated-game controller planning that composes movement policy rows, moving-platform handoff, copied-world dynamic push application, explicit distance-joint participation, invalid controller-body diagnostics, and replay-before/replay-after counters without mutating the caller world. PhysicsJoint3DStatus, PhysicsJoint3DDiagnostic, PhysicsDistanceJoint3DDesc, PhysicsJointSolve3DConfig, PhysicsJointSolve3DDesc, PhysicsJointSolve3DRow, PhysicsJointSolve3DResult, and solve_physics_joints_3d expose explicit first-party distance-joint solving with deterministic diagnostics, replay rows, invalid-request rejection, disabled-joint rows, velocity preservation, and no persistent world joint storage. PhysicsConstraint3DStatus, PhysicsConstraint3DDiagnostic, PhysicsConstraint3DKind, PhysicsFixedConstraint3DDesc, PhysicsLinearAxisConstraint3DDesc, PhysicsConstraintSolve3DConfig, PhysicsConstraintSolve3DDesc, PhysicsConstraintSolve3DRow, PhysicsConstraintSolve3DResult, and solve_physics_constraints_3d expose opt-in first-party distance/fixed/linear-axis positional constraint solving with deterministic row ordering, invalid-request diagnostics, disabled rows, inverse-mass correction, linear-axis limits, default-unbounded max_rows row budgets, row_budget_exceeded diagnostics, and no persistent world constraint storage. PhysicsKinematicMotion3DStatus, PhysicsKinematicMotion3DDiagnostic, PhysicsKinematicMotion3DRowKind, PhysicsKinematicMotion3DDesc, PhysicsKinematicMotion3DRow, PhysicsKinematicMotion3DResult, and plan_physics_kinematic_motion_3d expose value-only deterministic kinematic motion planning with slide, trigger-overlap, ground-probe, invalid-request, initial-overlap, and iteration-limit rows without persistent kinematic body storage. PhysicsSimpleVehicle3DStatus, PhysicsSimpleVehicle3DDiagnostic, PhysicsSimpleVehicle3DWheelDesc, PhysicsSimpleVehicle3DDesc, PhysicsSimpleVehicle3DWheelRow, PhysicsSimpleVehicle3DResult, and plan_physics_simple_vehicle_3d expose value-only simple vehicle policy planning that composes kinematic motion with deterministic wheel-probe rows without persistent vehicle bodies or drivetrain simulation. PhysicsDeterminismGate3DStatus, PhysicsDeterminismGate3DDiagnostic, PhysicsDeterminismGate3DConfig, PhysicsDeterminismGate3DCounts, PhysicsReplaySignature3D, PhysicsDeterminismGate3DResult, make_physics_replay_signature_3d, and evaluate_physics_determinism_gate_3d expose host-independent replay signatures and count-based budget gates over current 3D bodies, broadphase pairs, contacts, manifolds, trigger overlaps, and contact points. Iterative contact solver configuration with correction slop/percent controls, stacked-body coverage, replay-tested inverse-mass contact resolution, PhysicsCharacterController3DDesc and move_physics_character_controller_3d for conservative capsule movement/contact/grounding rows over PhysicsWorld3D::shape_sweep, PhysicsAuthoredCollisionScene3DDesc and build_physics_world_3d_from_authored_collision_scene for reviewed in-memory collision body rows with native backend requests failing closed. The earlier validated Physics 1.0 ready surface with explicit Jolt/native middleware exclusion from the default ready surface plus the optional `physics-jolt` adapter lane remains closed while this post-1.0 surface adds opt-in constraints, kinematic motion, and simple vehicle policy rows. Dynamic-vs-dynamic TOI, rotational CCD, rotational rigid-body constraints, 2D CCD, persistent joint assets, broad vehicle dynamics, persistent vehicle simulation, ragdolls, Jolt/native backends outside the default/generated-game 1.0 ready surface, except for the optional `physics-jolt` adapter lane, middleware, oriented boxes, mesh/convex casts, timing benchmarks, and broad physics beyond the first-party Physics 1.0 ready surface remain follow-up work.

modules.name: MK_physics_jolt

modules.name: MK_navigation
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only deterministic navigation foundation for generated games and gameplay tests. Currently provides NavigationGrid, NavigationGridCoord, NavigationPathRequest, NavigationPathResult, NavigationPathStatus, and find_navigation_path for bounds-checked cardinal grid A* pathfinding with walkability, positive traversal costs, stable tie-breaking, invalid/blocked endpoint diagnostics, no-path reporting, total path cost, and visited-node counts. Path Following / Steering Foundation v0 adds NavigationPoint2, NavigationPointPathBuildResult, build_navigation_point_path_result, build_navigation_point_path, NavigationPathFollowRequest, advance_navigation_path_following, NavigationArriveSteeringRequest, and calculate_navigation_arrive_steering for grid-path-to-point conversion, explicit path-follow cursor advancement, max-step/arrival-radius movement, remaining-distance reporting, and single-agent arrive steering. Navigation Agent Controller Foundation v0 adds NavigationAgentConfig, NavigationAgentState, NavigationAgentUpdateRequest, NavigationAgentUpdateResult, make_navigation_agent_state, replace_navigation_agent_path, cancel_navigation_agent_move, and update_navigation_agent for value-type idle/moving/reached/cancelled/invalid movement state, speed/delta clamping, final-destination slowing, path replacement, and desired velocity from actual path movement. Grid Dynamic Obstacle Replan v0 adds NavigationGridPathValidationRequest, NavigationGridPathValidationResult, NavigationGridPathValidationStatus, validate_navigation_grid_path, NavigationGridReplanRequest, NavigationGridReplanResult, NavigationGridReplanStatus, and replan_navigation_grid_path for validating remaining cardinal-grid paths against the current grid, reporting empty/start/goal/out-of-bounds/blocked/non-cardinal/unsupported-adjacency/cost-overflow diagnostics, reusing valid remaining paths, and recalculating deterministic replacement paths around newly blocked cells through find_navigation_path. Navigation Agent Local Avoidance v0 adds NavigationLocalAvoidanceRequest, NavigationLocalAvoidanceResult, NavigationLocalAvoidanceDiagnostic, and calculate_navigation_local_avoidance for finite-input single-agent velocity adjustment against explicit neighbor rows, duplicate-neighbor and calculation-overflow diagnostics, deterministic neighbor ordering, closest-approach prediction over the requested time window, overlap/future-collision separation, and max-speed clamping. Navigation Grid Path Smoothing v0 adds NavigationGridPathSmoothingRequest, NavigationGridPathSmoothingResult, NavigationGridPathSmoothingStatus, and smooth_navigation_grid_path for reducing validated cardinal grid paths through deterministic walkable line-of-sight while preserving start and goal waypoints. Navigation Production Path v1 adds NavigationGridAgentPathRequest, NavigationGridAgentPathPlan, NavigationGridAgentPathStatus, NavigationGridAgentPathDiagnostic, and plan_navigation_grid_agent_path to compose validated grid pathfinding, optional smoothing, point-path mapping, and NavigationAgentState creation for generated-game route setup. Navigation Navmesh Production Path v1 adds NavigationNavmeshPolygon, NavigationNavmeshPortal, NavigationNavmeshDynamicObstacle, NavigationNavmeshPathRequest, NavigationNavmeshPathResult, NavigationNavmeshPathStatus, NavigationNavmeshPathDiagnostic, and plan_navigation_navmesh_path for scene-ref polygon graph routing with dynamic obstacle blocking, deterministic diagnostics, point paths, visited counts, and cost evidence. Navigation Navmesh Crowd Query v1 adds NavigationCrowdAgentDesc, NavigationCrowdPlanRequest, NavigationCrowdAgentPlanRow, NavigationCrowdPlanResult, NavigationCrowdPlanStatus, NavigationCrowdPlanDiagnostic, and plan_navigation_navmesh_crowd for value-only deterministic crowd rows over navmesh routes, dynamic-obstacle participation, local-avoidance aggregation, max-agent budget rejection, duplicate/invalid-agent diagnostics, and stable replay counters. Navigation Hierarchical World v1 adds NavigationHierarchicalWorldRegion, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPortalEndpoint, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPortal, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPathRequest, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPathResult, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPortalPathRow, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPathStatus, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPathDiagnostic, and plan_navigation_hierarchical_world_path for value-only region graph routing over world-region refs, nav-data refs, portal ids, endpoint scene refs, deterministic region/portal paths, visited counts, and fail-closed malformed ref diagnostics. navmesh asset import, spline/funnel smoothing, persistent/full crowd simulation beyond value-only batch planning, RVO/ORCA, scene/physics integration, editor visualization, middleware, automatic nav baking, package streaming execution, and background navigation jobs remain future gameplay-system slices.

modules.name: MK_runtime_host

Currently provides DesktopGameRunner, DesktopRunConfig, DesktopRunResult, DesktopHostServices, and IDesktopEventPump so games can keep implementing mirakana::GameApp while a host drives IWindow lifecycle, VirtualInput, VirtualPointerInput, VirtualGamepadInput, VirtualLifecycle, optional renderer resize including swapchain-backed RhiFrameRenderer resize through IRenderer, optional DiagnosticsRecorder/IProfileClock frame instrumentation, backend-neutral renderer counter capture from IRenderer::stats(), and app update/stop status without exposing SDL, OS, RHI, or graphics API handles to game code. modules.name: MK_rhi
recentEvidence: RHI Native Async Upload Execution v1 adds execute_upload_gpu_batch_async plus RhiUploadGpuBatchExecutionResult so caller-owned RhiUploadStagingPlan/RhiUploadRing batches can prevalidate target counts and ring ownership, submit pending copies on a selected backend-neutral QueueKind, mark staging allocations with the returned FenceValue, and avoid helper-owned CPU waits without exposing native handles or claiming async overlap/performance.

Staging Pool Lease Adoption v1 adds RhiStagingBufferLease, RhiStagingBufferPool::try_acquire_lease, RhiStagingBufferPool::release(RhiStagingBufferLease), and RhiUploadRingDesc::buffer so explicit host-owned pool chunks can back caller-owned RhiUploadRing uploads without extra ring buffer allocation.
modules.purpose: Graphics-API-independent RHI contracts for backend capability/probe profiles, host support policy, shader artifact formats, backend bootstrap steps, buffers, textures including shared texture usage intent, samplers, swapchains, explicit swapchain frame acquire/release, backend-neutral swapchain resize, shaders, descriptor set layouts, descriptor sets, upload-buffer writes, copy/upload/readback commands with explicit texture footprints, CPU buffer readback, transient resource leases, presentation ownership and sequencing, single-pending-frame swapchain throttling, graphics pipelines with explicit vertex input layouts/attributes plus DepthStencilStateDesc depth-test/write/compare state, render passes with explicit clear colors plus optional RenderPassDepthAttachment and ClearDepthValue, descriptor set binding including sampler descriptors, vertex/index buffer binding, direct/indexed draw calls, resource transitions, texture aliasing barrier intent through IRhiCommandList::texture_aliasing_barrier and RhiStats::texture_aliasing_barriers for distinct texture handles plus public TextureHandle{} wildcard/null endpoints, including D3D12 backend-private null-resource aliasing barrier evidence tracked by DeviceContextStats::null_resource_aliasing_barriers, D3D12 transient texture lease counters through RhiStats::transient_texture_heap_allocations/RhiStats::transient_texture_placed_allocations/RhiStats::transient_texture_placed_resources_alive and DeviceContextStats::placed_resource_activation_barriers plus Vulkan transient alias-group allocation evidence through the same RhiStats counters, fences, backend-neutral write/copy/read/fence/sampler lifecycle stats, shared texture export/failure stats, and a NullRhiDevice for backend-free validation of depth attachment ownership, usage, format, clear-depth range, extent, and pipeline color/depth compatibility plus null_mark_buffer_released/null_mark_texture_released/null_mark_sampler_released/null_mark_descriptor_set_released/null_mark_descriptor_set_layout_released/null_mark_pipeline_layout_released helpers for deterministic safe-point invalidation during host-owned teardown validation. Renderer/RHI Resource Foundation v1 adds a foundation-only value API in resource_lifetime.hpp through RhiResourceLifetimeRegistry, generation-checked first-party resource handles, explicit resource kinds, owner labels, debug names, live/deferred-release records, queue-qualified fence retirement, deterministic diagnostics, and enum-backed marker-style lifetime event rows. Upload/Staging Foundation v1 adds RhiUploadStagingPlan in upload_staging.hpp with staging allocation ids, buffer and texture copy records, deterministic copy-range diagnostics, submitted FenceValue tracking, and completed-fence retirement. Upload ring and staging pool execution v1 adds RhiUploadRing fence-tracked suballocations over one copy_source upload buffer, RhiStagingBufferPool freelists, RhiStagingBufferLease, RhiStagingBufferPool::try_acquire_lease, RhiStagingBufferPool::release(RhiStagingBufferLease), RhiUploadRingDesc::buffer, validate_upload_gpu_batch, record_upload_gpu_batch, and mark_pending_allocations_submitted for IRhiCommandList buffer and buffer-to-texture copies with null-backend readback proof. These foundations do not migrate native backend destruction, implement vendor-specialized async GPU upload queues or streaming package integration, a GPU allocator, residency budgets, package streaming, GPU markers beyond the scoped API, editor resource management/capture tooling execution beyond reviewed request handoff, production renderer scheduling, or 2D/3D playable vertical-slice readiness. The D3D12 IRhiDevice bridge implements private DSV-backed public depth attachments, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth format validation, readback-observable depth-test proof, sampled-depth proof, depth-aware postprocess proof, and directional shadow receiver proof; the Vulkan bridge implements private depth-aspect image views for sampled descriptors, depth|stencil D24S8 barrier aspect masks, required synchronization2 feature query/device enablement, depth_write synchronization2 barriers, dynamic-rendering depth attachments, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth format validation, environment-gated depth-ordered readback proof, environment-gated visible sampled-depth readback proof, environment-gated postprocess-depth readback proof, and environment-gated directional shadow receiver readback proof while keeping Vulkan handles private.

modules.name: MK_scene
modules.purpose: Scene graph foundation for editor hierarchy and runtime scene loading. Currently provides rotation-aware transforms, parent/child relationships, camera/light/mesh/sprite renderer components, backend-neutral 2D playable scene validation through mirakana::validate_playable_2d_scene for primary orthographic camera, visible sprite, and visible mesh rejection diagnostics, renderer-neutral render packet extraction with visible meshes, visible sprites, world transforms, scene-subtree prefab creation, prefab instantiation, PrefabInstantiateDesc path metadata, ScenePrefabSourceLink / SceneNode::prefab_source source identity rows for instantiated prefab nodes, deterministic prefab text serialization, dependency-free prefab variant/override value types with diagnostics, composition into plain PrefabDefinition, deterministic GameEngine.PrefabVariant text serialization, and deterministic scene text serialization that preserves component data plus source-link rows. Scene/Component/Prefab Schema is implemented as a contract-only public MK_scene data spine through stable AuthoringId values, schema-driven SceneComponentDocument rows, deterministic validation diagnostics, GameEngine.Scene serialization/deserialization including SceneNodePrefabSource / SceneComponentPrefabSource prefab_source provenance rows for copied prefab node/component sources, GameEngine.Prefab validation/serialization/deserialization, stable prefab override paths for node name/parent and component property overrides, non-mutating stable-id prefab instance refresh planning through ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshPlan, ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshRow, and plan_scene_prefab_instance_refresh comparing selected instance-root provenance against PrefabDocument source_node_id / source_component_id rows and failing closed with duplicate_prefab_source_identity diagnostics for duplicate copied source identities, unsupported_nested_prefab_instance diagnostics for descendant nested prefab roots, unsupported_local_prefab_child diagnostics for local child nodes, and unsupported_local_prefab_component diagnostics for local components in the selected subtree, plus pure value stable-id prefab instance refresh apply through ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshResult and apply_scene_prefab_instance_refresh preserving matched node/component ids and state, adding deterministic instance_root/refresh/<source-id> rows, removing stale sourced rows, and returning source-to-result mappings without file writes, editor actions, AI command execution, runtime prefab semantics, or nested propagation. Runtime package migration, editor productization, nested prefab propagation/merge resolution UX, production 2D atlas/tilemap/native GPU output, and ready 3D playable vertical slices remain follow-up work.

display path diagnostics, viewport toolbar, Play/Pause/Stop controls, timeline playback controls and event table adapted from MK_editor_ui models, recorder-backed Profiler panel that records graphics.notes: Runtime owners and the IRhiDevice bridge cover backend-neutral resource/shader/descriptor/pipeline/swapchain creation, standalone texture draw/readback, swapchain clear/present, command submission, fence stats, readback-domain buffer reads, private depth image-view/layout/barrier/dynamic-rendering depth attachments, required synchronization2 feature enablement, D24S8 depth|stencil image-barrier aspects, and actual surface capability/format/present-mode queries before native swapchain creation. SDL3/Vulkan game-window RhiFrameRenderer creation, host-owned cooked-scene GPU binding injection, depth-aware Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 package proof from precompiled SPIR-V artifacts with postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, package-visible Directional Shadow Filtering v0 proof with target-specific shadow receiver/shadow SPIR-V artifacts, directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, framegraph_passes=3, framegraph_passes_executed=6, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=6, and framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=15, plus Renderer Package Quality Gates v1 validation with --require-renderer-quality-gates, renderer_quality_status=ready, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_render_passes=6, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_barrier_steps=15, renderer_quality_framegraph_render_passes_ok=1, renderer_quality_framegraph_barrier_steps_ok=1, and renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1 is validated on this Windows host when Vulkan runtime, surface support, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen, and spirv-val are available; Linux and generated-game Vulkan availability remain host/toolchain gated. The visible draw/readback, texture-sampling, runtime material sampling, cooked-scene palette, depth-ordered draw/readback, sampled-depth readback, postprocess-depth readback, and shadow receiver readback proofs are environment-gated by local Vulkan runtime plus real SPIR-V artifacts.

graphics.name: metal

physics.name: first-party-deterministic
physics.notes: 2D/3D broadphase, circle/sphere/capsule contacts, layers/masks, collision-bounds raycasts, trigger overlaps, conservative bounds-based shape sweeps, exact 3D AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule shape sweeps against current AABB/sphere/capsule targets through PhysicsWorld3D::exact_shape_sweep, exact sphere convenience casts, deterministic contact manifolds through PhysicsContactPoint3D, PhysicsContactManifold3D, and PhysicsWorld3D::contact_manifolds with stable feature ids, warm-start-safe point rows, trigger/disabled exclusion, and solver consumption for current AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule contacts, conservative 3D capsule character-controller movement, first-party in-memory and package-authored collision rows, iterative solver configuration, stacked-body coverage, replay tests, host-independent PhysicsReplaySignature3D rows, and count-based PhysicsDeterminismGate3DResult budget diagnostics through make_physics_replay_signature_3d and evaluate_physics_determinism_gate_3d plus opt-in deterministic PhysicsWorld3D::step_continuous CCD rows, pure generated-game character/dynamic policy rows through PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DDesc, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DRow, PhysicsCharacterDynamicPolicy3DResult, and evaluate_physics_character_dynamic_policy_3d, and explicit distance-joint solving through PhysicsJointSolve3DDesc, PhysicsJointSolve3DResult, and solve_physics_joints_3d define the validated first-party Physics 1.0 ready surface; opt-in distance/fixed/linear-axis positional constraint solving through PhysicsConstraintSolve3DDesc, PhysicsConstraintSolve3DResult, and solve_physics_constraints_3d is a post-1.0 first-party expansion; dynamic-vs-dynamic TOI, rotational CCD, rotational rigid-body constraints, 2D CCD, persistent joint assets, vehicles, ragdolls, Jolt/native backends outside the 1.0 ready surface, middleware, oriented boxes, mesh/convex casts, timing benchmarks, and broad physics beyond the first-party Physics 1.0 ready surface are future work.

network.name: first-party-loopback-facade
network.notes: Runtime Network Transport Adapter Facade v1 validates one loopback-only caller-owned adapter exchange through RuntimeNetworkLoopbackExchangeRequest, IRuntimeNetworkTransportAdapter, and execute_runtime_network_loopback_exchange. The facade fails closed before dispatch for missing or unavailable adapters, unsupported loopback/reliable/unreliable capabilities, native-handle requests, peer_count != 1, invalid channel counts/channels, payloads above the first-party ceiling, invalid service budgets, and adapter exceptions. MK_runtime does not open sockets, own native handles, implement encryption/authentication, matchmaking, NAT traversal, replication, rollback, or claim broad multiplayer readiness.

network.name: enet-loopback
network.notes: Optional ENet adapter lane behind MK_ENABLE_NETWORK_ENET and the vcpkg network-enet feature. It keeps ENet private to engine/runtime/network/enet implementation sources, supports one local loopback client peer with reliable and unreliable packet rows, validates install/export through Mirakanai_HAS_NETWORK_ENET, and is not external network readiness, secure transport, matchmaking, NAT traversal, replication, rollback, or broad multiplayer readiness.

platform.name: headless
platform.notes: Virtual input, lifecycle, mobile lifecycle/touch/orientation/safe-area/storage/permission contracts, filesystem, process, file watcher selection, and deterministic memory/rooted services are the generated-game baseline.

platform.name: sdl3-desktop
platform.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate-desktop-game-runtime.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/build-gui.ps1
platform.notes: Optional desktop window/input/clipboard/file-dialog/cursor/lifecycle adapter for the editor and the first desktop game runtime shell. Game runtime event polling should go through MK_runtime_host_sdl3 so SDL_PollEvent stays out of game code.

platform.name: mobile-contracts
platform.validation: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1
platform.notes: SDK-independent mobile lifecycle, touch, orientation, safe-area, storage, and permission contracts are implemented; Android GameActivity and Apple package templates adapt into these contracts without exposing native handles.

importerCapabilities.runtimePolicy: Game runtime code consumes cooked artifacts and must not parse external source formats directly.

packagingTargets.name: source-tree-default
packagingTargets.notes: Builds and tests the engine and sample games from the source tree.

packagingTargets.name: desktop-gui
packagingTargets.notes: Builds the optional SDL3, Dear ImGui, editor, and SDL3 audio adapter lane.

packagingTargets.name: desktop-game-runtime
packagingTargets.notes: Builds the optional editor-independent SDL3 desktop game runtime samples through the SDL3-only desktop-runtime preset, plus SDL3 platform/audio tests, runtime host tests including DiagnosticsRecorder-backed frame profiles and renderer counter capture, SDL3 event-pump tests, reusable SdlDesktopGameHost tests, SDL3 presentation fallback/report tests including scene GPU binding unavailable, postprocess request validation, directional shadow request/filter metadata validation, Renderer Package Quality Gates v1 evaluation, and Vulkan request diagnostics, manifest-linked generated registered-game package metadata including manifest-derived package file source/install paths and target-specific shader artifact paths, finite sample_desktop_runtime_shell --smoke, DXC-available build-output DXIL artifact smoke paths, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen-available build-output SPIR-V artifact smoke paths, the non-shell sample_desktop_runtime_game --smoke path, the config-only sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package scaffold proof generated by tools/new-game.ps1 -Template DesktopRuntimePackage, the sample_generated_desktop_runtime_cooked_scene_package scaffold proof generated by tools/new-game.ps1 -Template DesktopRuntimeCookedScenePackage with --require-scene-package, and the DXC-available sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package_shader_artifacts_smoke source-tree proof generated by tools/new-game.ps1 -Template DesktopRuntimeMaterialShaderPackage with modern material package counters emitted by the generated main.cpp. The reusable host gives games selecting desktop-game-runtime a first-party SDL3 windowed host path without copying sample host assembly; the samples use SdlDesktopGameHost and SdlDesktopPresentation for host-owned renderer selection with D3D12/Vulkan-ready surface probing, optional build-output D3D12 DXIL or Vulkan SPIR-V shader bytecode RhiFrameRenderer creation, host-owned packaged D3D12/Vulkan scene GPU binding, host-owned Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 setup including optional depth-input fallback/report diagnostics, host-owned package-visible fixed sampled-depth 3x3 PCF directional shadow filtering setup with first-party fallback/report diagnostics, package-visible renderer quality summary fields such as renderer_quality_status, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_render_passes, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_barrier_steps, renderer_quality_framegraph_render_passes_ok, renderer_quality_framegraph_barrier_steps_ok, and renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok, package-visible modern material counters such as modern_material_variants, modern_material_ready, modern_material_diagnostics, modern_material_texture_dependencies, modern_material_shader_evidence_ready, modern_material_d3d12_shader_evidence_ready, modern_material_vulkan_shader_evidence_ready, and modern_material_selected_shader_evidence_ready, backend-neutral presentation report fields including framegraph_passes_executed, framegraph_render_passes_recorded, and framegraph_barrier_steps_executed, and NullRenderer fallback diagnostics; DesktopGameRunner can drive swapchain-backed RhiFrameRenderer resize and optional observability capture through public IWindow/IRenderer/MK_core contracts.

packagingTargets.name: desktop-runtime-release
packagingTargets.notes: Builds the SDL3-only desktop-runtime-release preset, cleans the desktop runtime install prefix, installs a selected registered desktop runtime game executable plus selected package files derived from target metadata, selected D3D12 DXIL and explicitly requested Vulkan SPIR-V shader artifacts when declared, SDL3.dll on Windows, share/Mirakanai/desktop-runtime-games.json package metadata with selectedPackageTarget, gameManifest paths, packageFiles, d3d12ShaderArtifacts, and vulkanShaderArtifacts, validates the installed SDK consumer, runs the installed selected game smoke from metadata, verifies the selected installed game manifest, package files, required shader artifacts, and presentation/postprocess/directional-shadow/renderer-quality status fields, and creates a CPack ZIP without requiring Dear ImGui or MK_editor. pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 defaults to sample_desktop_runtime_shell with metadata-driven --smoke --require-d3d12-shaders --require-vulkan-shaders; tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package validates the generated config-only package scaffold with metadata-driven --smoke --require-config runtime/sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package.config and no shader/cooked-scene claims; tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget sample_desktop_runtime_game validates the non-shell packaged scene proof with metadata-driven --smoke --require-config runtime/sample_desktop_runtime_game.config --require-scene-package runtime/sample_desktop_runtime_game.geindex --require-d3d12-scene-shaders --video-driver windows --require-d3d12-renderer --require-scene-gpu-bindings --require-postprocess --require-postprocess-depth-input --require-directional-shadow --require-directional-shadow-filtering --require-d3d12-shadow-cascade-policy --require-lighting-shadow-policy --require-scene-scale-policy --require-d3d12-instanced-draw-evidence --require-renderer-quality-gates --require-framegraph-multiqueue-evidence --require-native-ui-overlay --require-native-ui-textured-sprite-atlas --require-gpu-skinning --require-quaternion-animation, including cooked scene package load, renderer-submit counters, target-specific scene/postprocess/shadow DXIL artifacts, host-owned D3D12 scene GPU binding counters, Frame Graph/Postprocess v0 status with postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, Directional Shadow Filtering v0 status with directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, framegraph_passes=3, framegraph_passes_executed=6, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=6, and framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=15, Scene Scale Policy v1 selected D3D12 package fields with scene_scale_policy_backend_instancing_evidence_required=1 and scene_scale_policy_backend_instancing_evidence_ready=1, selected D3D12 instanced draw execution fields with d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_status=ready, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_ready=1, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_selected=1, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_expected_instances=6, positive d3d12_instanced_draw_calls, positive d3d12_instanced_indexed_draw_calls, positive d3d12_instanced_instances_submitted, positive rhi_instanced_draw_calls, positive rhi_instanced_indexed_draw_calls, positive rhi_instanced_instances_submitted, d3d12_instanced_draws_ok=1, d3d12_instanced_instances_ok=1, and Renderer Package Quality Gates v1 status with renderer_quality_status=ready, renderer_quality_ready=1, renderer_quality_diagnostics=0, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_passes=3, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_render_passes=6, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_barrier_steps=15, renderer_quality_framegraph_render_passes_ok=1, renderer_quality_framegraph_barrier_steps_ok=1, and renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1. The same selected target can validate target-specific scene/postprocess/shadow SPIR-V artifacts and host-owned Vulkan scene GPU binding plus depth-aware postprocess/directional shadow filtering with postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, directional_shadow_ready=1, directional_shadow_filter_mode=fixed_pcf_3x3, directional_shadow_filter_taps=9, directional_shadow_filter_radius_texels=1, framegraph_passes_executed=6, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=6, framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=15, renderer_quality_status=ready, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_render_passes=6, renderer_quality_framegraph_render_passes_ok=1, renderer_quality_framegraph_barrier_steps_ok=1, renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok=1, and -RequireVulkanShaders plus explicit Vulkan smoke args on a ready Windows/Vulkan host.

packagingTargets.name: asset-importers
packagingTargets.notes: Builds MK_tools with optional libspng, fastgltf, and miniaudio importer adapters and runs importer-capable tests.

packagingTargets.name: windows-cpack-zip
packagingTargets.notes: Builds Release, runs Release tests, installs the SDK layout, validates examples/installed_consumer against the installed mirakana:: package, and creates a CPack ZIP with package metadata.

packagingTargets.name: android-gameactivity
packagingTargets.notes: Android Gradle Plugin 9.1.0, Gradle 9.3.1, JDK 17, Android SDK Platform 36.1, Build Tools 36.0.0, Android SDK CMake 4.1.2, NDK CMake, Prefab, AndroidX AppCompat 1.7.1, AndroidX Core 1.18.0, GameActivity 3.0.4, NDK Vulkan loader linkage, private ANativeWindow/VK_KHR_android_surface creation bridge, private low-latency AAudio float32 output adapter, generated asset packaging, host-validated Debug/Release APK output, Release signing verification with non-repository upload keys, upload certificate export, deterministic ANDROID_AVD_HOME normalization, AVD readiness diagnostics, and emulator install/launch smoke coverage exist; generated game manifests are validated and packaged before package build attempts, and pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1 reports signing/toolchain/AVD/device blockers explicitly with stable kind slugs.

packagingTargets.name: ios-metal
packagingTargets.notes: Apple CMake/Xcode iOS bundle template exists with UIKit/Metal framework linkage, Metal-backed root view setup, sandbox save/cache/shared storage root mapping, game manifest resource packaging, bundle identifier inputs, simulator/device SDK selection, simulator signing suppression, Xcode signing inputs, iOS Simulator install/launch smoke scripting through pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/smoke-ios-package.ps1, and explicit full-Xcode/iOS-SDK/iOS-runtime blockers with stable kind slugs from pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1; actual host-validated simulator/device results and backend-neutral Metal presentation remain Apple-host gated.

validationRecipes.name: default
validationRecipes.purpose: Run the repository default correctness gate before reporting completion.

validationRecipes.name: agent-contract
validationRecipes.command: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-ai-integration.ps1; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-json-contracts.ps1
validationRecipes.purpose: Validate AI instructions, manifests, schemas, and generated context.

validationRecipes.name: public-api-boundary
validationRecipes.purpose: Check public headers and backend interop boundaries after public API or handle-boundary changes.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-gui
validationRecipes.purpose: Build and test optional SDL3, Dear ImGui, editor, and SDL3 audio adapter code.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-game-runtime
validationRecipes.purpose: Build and test optional SDL3 desktop game runtime host code through the SDL3-only desktop-runtime preset, including manifest-linked generated registered-game metadata with package file path validation, SDL3 platform/audio tests, runtime host tests, the SDL event pump, reusable SdlDesktopGameHost tests, SDL presentation fallback/required-bytecode tests, finite sample_desktop_runtime_shell smoke execution, DXC-available build-output DXIL shader artifact smoke, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen-available build-output SPIR-V shader artifact smoke, non-shell sample_desktop_runtime_game smoke execution, sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package config-only scaffold smoke execution, sample_generated_desktop_runtime_cooked_scene_package cooked-scene scaffold smoke execution, sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package material/shader scaffold smoke execution, and DXC-available sample_generated_desktop_runtime_material_shader_package_shader_artifacts_smoke source-tree shader-artifact execution. Real-window SDL3/D3D12 or SDL3/Vulkan renderer smokes remain explicit local commands because they open a native desktop window.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-release-package
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the optional Release desktop runtime package, install the selected registered runtime game executable, SDL3 runtime DLL, selected package files, generated desktop-runtime game package metadata with gameManifest and packageFiles paths derived from manifest-driven or literal registration, optional generated D3D12 DXIL and Vulkan SPIR-V shader artifacts for the current sample shell shader-artifact package path, validate installed SDK metadata, validate a clean installed consumer, validate the installed selected game manifest, package files, shader artifacts, and smoke from metadata, including selected-target cooked package load and renderer-submit proofs when the game metadata requires them, and create the CPack package artifact.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-shell-vulkan-window-smoke
validationRecipes.purpose: On a Windows host with Vulkan runtime, surface support, and generated SPIR-V artifacts, validate the SDL3/Vulkan game-window RhiFrameRenderer path without exposing Vulkan or SDL handles to gameplay.

validationRecipes.name: desktop-runtime-generated-scaffold-package
validationRecipes.purpose: Build the selected generated desktop runtime package scaffold lane and validate installed sample_generated_desktop_runtime_package with manifest-derived runtime config package files, metadata-selected --require-config smoke args, and deterministic NullRenderer fallback without D3D12/Vulkan shader artifacts or cooked-scene claims.

safePointPackageReplacementLoops.summary: Host-owned safe-point package replacement and unload execution for preloaded runtime packages. Replacement rebuilds a RuntimeResourceCatalog from the pending package, commits the RuntimeAssetPackageStore and catalog together only at a safe point, and rolls back invalid pending packages before active swap. Unload clears the active package and commits an empty catalog only at a safe point, discarding pending replacement state when an active package exists. Both paths report deterministic generation and stale-handle invalidation counters without executing async streaming, eviction, renderer/RHI teardown, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, or renderer quality claims.

Duplicate or invalid package records keep the existing active package/catalog live and clear the pending package.

Clear the active package and replace the catalog with an empty generation only when an active package exists; pending replacements are discarded on successful unload and preserved when no active package exists.

recipes.summary: Host-independent renderer planning contract for deterministic 2D SpriteCommand batch plans and selected world/UI/effects budget profiles. recipes.followUpCapability: Use this as a planning prerequisite only. Generated 3D scaffolds may decode a cooked scalar animation clip via runtime_animation_float_clip_payload and apply binding rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance data through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_float_clip. Generated 3D scaffolds may also decode cooked morph_mesh_cpu rows via runtime_morph_mesh_cpu_payload, sample morph-weight clips through sample_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu_animation_float_clip for CPU morph package smoke counters, decode cooked quaternion clips via runtime_animation_quaternion_clip_payload, convert byte rows with make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes, sample AnimationPose3d through sample_animation_local_pose_3d for quaternion package smoke counters, run selected --require-gameplay-systems smoke through public MK_physics authored collision/controller, MK_navigation route planning/ticks, MK_ai perception/blackboard/behavior tree, MK_audio device stream rendering, MK_animation state transitions, scene gameplay binding readiness through gameplay_systems_scene_binding_* fields, and scene gameplay interaction rows through gameplay_systems_scene_interaction_* fields, run selected --require-scene-collision-package smoke through cooked GameEngine.PhysicsCollisionScene3D rows, runtime_physics_collision_scene_3d_payload, and build_physics_world_3d_from_runtime_collision_scene, use explicit D3D12 scene mesh-to-morph bindings for package-visible POSITION-delta morph draw counters, run the selected --require-shadow-morph-composition package smoke to validate renderer_gpu_morph_draws, renderer_morph_descriptor_binds, directional_shadow_ready=1, framegraph_passes=3, framegraph_passes_executed=6, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=6, and framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=15 on the shifted directional shadow receiver path, run the selected --require-visible-3d-production-proof package smoke to validate visible_3d_* aggregate counters over D3D12 scene GPU, postprocess, renderer-quality, playable, gameplay-system, and native UI overlay readiness, and run selected --require-native-ui-overlay / --require-native-ui-textured-sprite-atlas / --require-native-ui-text-glyph-atlas package smokes to validate MK_ui HUD box submission plus cooked GameEngine.UiAtlas image sprite and text glyph submission through the host-owned native UI overlay path. Source material/HLSL, morph source, animation source, quaternion animation source, Scene/Prefab files, and UI atlas metadata files remain authoring or cooked package inputs, and runtime source asset parsing remains unsupported.

commandSurfaces.summary: Dry-run or apply a reviewed DesktopRuntime2DPackage or DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffold from a fail-closed aiWorkflow.gameDesignSpec descriptor through tools/create-game-recipe.ps1.

requestModes.notes: Dry-run validates the reviewed design spec, calls tools/new-game.ps1 -DryRun with fixed arguments, and returns plannedFiles/plannedChanges without creating games/<game_name>/.

Apply repeats the design-spec validation and new-game dry-run preflight, calls tools/new-game.ps1 with fixed arguments, then replaces the generated manifest gameDesignSpec with the reviewed input descriptor.

requestShape.pathPolicy: Repository-relative DesignSpecPath only; generated roots are fixed to games/<game_name>/ and games/CMakeLists.txt through tools/new-game.ps1.

capabilityGates.notes: Create-game-recipe may generate reviewed 2D package scaffolds only when the matching design spec selects DesktopRuntime2DPackage.
Create-game-recipe may generate reviewed 3D package scaffolds only when the matching design spec selects DesktopRuntime3DPackage.
Apply must preserve a fail-closed aiWorkflow.gameDesignSpec row in the generated game.agent.json.

undoToken.notes: The command result describes manual revert guidance; persistent undo-token storage remains outside this foundation slice.

commandSurfaces.notes: Reviewed tooling slice only: no arbitrary shell execution, no autonomous game design, no external asset generation, and no package cooking beyond the deterministic scaffold produced by tools/new-game.ps1.

commandSurfaces.summary: Dry-run or apply a new GameEngine.Scene scene document through the reviewed Scene/Prefab authoring helper.

requestModes.notes: Dry-run uses plan_scene_prefab_authoring to validate stable authoring ids and component rows, then reports changedFiles and modelMutations without writing.
Apply uses apply_scene_prefab_authoring and writes only validated authored .scene files after the same plan succeeds.

requestShape.pathPolicy: Only safe repository-relative authored GameEngine.Scene .scene paths are accepted; runtime cooked package paths are not written by this command.

capabilityGates.notes: The command is limited to the contract-only MK_scene authoring model.

undoToken.notes: Undo tokens are placeholders; the helper reports deterministic changed files but not full editor history entries.

commandSurfaces.notes: Ready Scene/Prefab authoring surface through plan_scene_prefab_authoring and apply_scene_prefab_authoring for GameEngine.Scene and GameEngine.Prefab documents. The helper does not evaluate arbitrary shell, and free-form edits are not supported. Scene runtime package migration is handled by the separate migrate-scene-runtime-package reviewed bridge; editor productization, nested prefab merge/resolution UX, source asset import execution, package cooking, renderer/RHI residency, package streaming, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, and broad renderer quality remain unsupported or host-gated.

commandSurfaces.summary: Dry-run or apply a first-party GameEngine.Scene .scene payload plus matching cooked package index scene, mesh, material, and sprite dependency rows.

requestModes.notes: Dry-run uses plan_scene_package_update to serialize GameEngine.Scene, update an in-memory .geindex, verify AssetKind::scene with scene_mesh, scene_material, and scene_sprite dependency rows, and report changed files without writing.
Apply uses apply_scene_package_update and writes the .scene and .geindex only after the same validation succeeds.

requestShape.pathPolicy: packageIndexPath, sceneOutputPath, and referenced package rows must be package-relative safe paths; mesh/material/sprite dependencies must already exist as cooked package rows of the expected kind.

capabilityGates.notes: Scene payloads must use first-party MK_scene contracts while create-scene remains authored-only.
Scene mesh/material/sprite references must use stable asset identity rows.
Agent-facing command surface promotion must keep agent-check green.

undoToken.notes: The helper reports deterministic changed files, but structured undo tokens remain placeholders.

commandSurfaces.summary: Dry-run or apply a reviewed bridge from authored GameEngine.Scene plus GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry rows into the existing GameEngine.Scene runtime package update surface.

requestModes.notes: Dry-run uses plan_scene_runtime_package_migration to parse GameEngine.Scene, resolve mesh/material/sprite keys through GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry, populate modelMutations placement_rows through plan_asset_identity_placements, call plan_scene_package_update, and report deterministic changedFiles, modelMutations, validationRecipes, unsupportedGapIds, and placeholder undoToken without writing.

requestShape.pathPolicy: scenePath and sourceRegistryPath must be safe repository-relative paths ending in .scene and .geassets; packageIndexPath and outputScenePath must be both safe repository-relative and package-relative paths ending in .geindex and .scene; empty, absolute, drive-colon, backslash, control-character, semicolon, '.', and '..' segments are rejected.

capabilityGates.notes: Authored GameEngine.Scene validation and component rows are required.
GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry and stable AssetKey projection are required.
The migration reuses the existing GameEngine.Scene package update surface.
Agent-facing command surface promotion must keep agent-check green.

commandSurfaces.notes: Ready narrow Scene runtime package migration through plan_scene_runtime_package_migration and apply_scene_runtime_package_migration. It maps supported GameEngine.Scene camera/light/mesh_renderer/sprite_renderer properties, including light inner_cone_radians and outer_cone_radians, and GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry mesh/material/texture keys into GameEngine.Scene content, emits SceneRuntimePackageMigrationModelMutation placement_rows through plan_asset_identity_placements for scene package, mesh renderer, and sprite renderer references, then reuses plan_scene_package_update and apply_scene_package_update for the .scene and .geindex writes. It requires safe repository-relative and package-relative paths, does not evaluate arbitrary shell, and free-form edits are not supported. external importer execution is not supported; package cooking, dependent asset cooking, renderer/RHI residency, package streaming, material graph, shader graph, live shader generation, editor productization, Metal readiness, public native/RHI handles, and general production renderer quality remain unsupported or host-gated.

commandSurfaces.summary: Dry-run or execute reviewed non-mutating runtime scene package validation for an explicit .geindex package and Scene AssetKey through MK_runtime and MK_runtime_scene.

Execute uses execute_runtime_scene_package_validation to call mirakana::runtime::load_runtime_asset_package and mirakana::runtime_scene::instantiate_runtime_scene, then reports packageSummary, sceneSummary, references, packageRecordCount, diagnostics, validationRecipes, unsupportedGapIds, and placeholder undoToken without mutating files.

requestShape.pathPolicy: packageIndexPath must be a safe package-relative .geindex path; contentRoot must be empty or a safe package-relative path and is prepended to cooked package entry paths; omit contentRoot when .geindex entries already include the runtime-relative directory. Empty, absolute, drive-colon, backslash, control-character, semicolon, '.', and '..' segments are rejected.
