authoringSurfaces.notes: Headless, DesktopRuntimePackage, DesktopRuntimeCookedScenePackage, DesktopRuntimeMaterialShaderPackage, DesktopRuntime2DPackage, and DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds are validated by agent/static checks and focused desktop runtime/package recipes.

Game manifests expose backend readiness, importer requirements, packaging targets, runtimePackageFiles, runtimeSceneValidationTargets, atlasTilemapAuthoringTargets, prefabScenePackageAuthoringTargets, registeredSourceAssetCookTargets, packageStreamingResidencyTargets, and validation recipes for generated games. runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows are descriptor-only inputs for validate-runtime-scene-package; atlasTilemapAuthoringTargets rows are deterministic data-only 2D tilemap package authoring descriptors; prefabScenePackageAuthoringTargets rows are deterministic 3D Scene/Prefab plus selected SourceAssetRegistry package authoring descriptors; registeredSourceAssetCookTargets rows are descriptor-only cook-registered-source-assets inputs paired to prefab authoring rows with explicit or registry-closure dependency expansion; packageStreamingResidencyTargets rows are selected host-gated safe-point package streaming descriptors and do not add shell commands, runtime source parsing, renderer/RHI residency execution, broad background streaming, arbitrary eviction, editor productization, or native handles.
GUI-independent v0 authoring models exist, but they are not yet the future stable-id Scene/Component/Prefab Schema command layer.

Contract-only stable-id scene/component/prefab v2 value APIs, validation, deterministic text IO, stable prefab override path composition, deterministic prefab source provenance rows through SceneNodePrefabSource / SceneComponentPrefabSource `prefab_source` text fields, non-mutating stable-id prefab instance refresh planning through ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshPlan, ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshRow, and plan_scene_prefab_instance_refresh comparing a selected instance root against PrefabDocument source_node_id / source_component_id rows, fail-closed duplicate_prefab_source_identity diagnostics for duplicate copied source identities, unsupported_nested_prefab_instance diagnostics for descendant nested prefab roots, unsupported_local_prefab_child / unsupported_local_prefab_component diagnostics for local selected-subtree rows without selected-prefab provenance, and pure value stable-id prefab instance refresh apply through ScenePrefabInstanceRefreshResult and apply_scene_prefab_instance_refresh are implemented in MK_scene. The value apply preserves matched node/component ids and state, adds deterministic instance_root/refresh/<source-id> rows, removes stale sourced rows, returns source-to-result mappings, and does not create editor actions, implement runtime prefab semantics, or perform nested propagation. Reviewed MK_tools dry-run/apply authoring commands now operate on this contract, instantiate-prefab records source prefab path plus source node/component ids, refresh-prefab-instance wraps the stable-id value apply into a reviewed safe .scene write surface, and the separate migrate-scene-runtime-package surface bridges supported Scene rows into the existing Scene package update helper. A focused command-authored workflow proof now drives apply_source_asset_registration -> apply_registered_source_asset_cook_package -> apply_scene_runtime_package_migration -> execute_runtime_scene_package_validation, including perspective camera and directional light cone-field projection. Source asset registration is ready through GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry. Editor migration, nested prefab propagation/merge resolution UX, broad/dependent package cooking, and 2D/3D vertical slices remain follow-up work.

Runtime Scene Gameplay Binding v1 is a backend-neutral value contract for generated gameplay authoring: RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingSourceRow rows carry binding_id, gameplay_system_id, slot_id, node_name, and a required RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingComponentKind, and resolve_runtime_scene_gameplay_bindings maps them to RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingRow node ids only when authored scene names are unique and required camera, light, mesh_renderer, sprite_renderer, or any_renderable component gates pass. Diagnostics cover invalid ids, duplicate binding ids, missing or duplicate node names, and missing required components; failed resolutions clear partial bindings. Selected 2D/3D package smokes expose gameplay_systems_scene_binding_ready and exact source/binding/system/component row counters before generated-game quality gates treat scene wiring as evidenced. This is not a gameplay system scheduler, script runtime, editor command surface, renderer/RHI execution path, package format change, or broad generated-game production readiness claim.

Runtime Scene Gameplay Interaction Framework v1 is a backend-neutral value contract for generated gameplay authoring: RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionSourceRow rows carry action_id, RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionKind, source_binding_id, target_binding_id, objective_id, and amount; RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionPlanRequest carries the current RuntimeSceneGameplaySessionState; and plan_runtime_scene_gameplay_interactions consumes resolved RuntimeSceneGameplayBindingRow values to produce deterministic RuntimeSceneGameplayInteractionPlan rows for trigger, pickup, damage, heal, objective_progress, objective_complete, win, loss, and restart interactions. Diagnostics cover invalid or duplicate action ids, missing source or target bindings, missing objective ids, invalid positive amount requirements, and rejected terminal-state transitions; failed plans clear partial rows and preserve the caller-provided session state. This is not a gameplay system scheduler, script runtime, physics/event dispatcher, editor command surface, renderer/RHI execution path, package format change, or broad generated-game production readiness claim.

Runtime Session Profile Path Policy v1 is a backend-neutral value contract for generated game save/settings/profile placement: RuntimeSessionProfilePathRequest carries a safe game_id, profile_id, and project-relative root_path; plan_runtime_session_profile_paths returns deterministic save.gesave, settings.settings, and input.geinputprofile paths under one game-local profile root; and RuntimeSessionProfilePathPlan diagnostics fail closed for invalid game ids, profile ids, root paths, absolute paths, parent traversal, path separators in ids, and control characters. This is not a platform user-directory resolver, cloud save system, binary save format, native handle surface, filesystem mutation helper, or broad save migration/backup workflow.

Runtime Session Profile Document Bundle v1 layers deterministic save/settings/input-profile document load and reset/write helpers over Runtime Session Profile Path Policy v1: RuntimeSessionProfileDocuments carries RuntimeSaveData, RuntimeSettings, and RuntimeInputRebindingProfile values; load_runtime_session_profile_documents returns RuntimeSessionProfileDocumentRow rows with loaded, defaulted_missing, failed_corrupt, failed_unsupported_version, and failed_invalid_path statuses; and write_runtime_session_profile_documents validates all three documents before writing reviewed save.gesave, settings.settings, and input.geinputprofile defaults. Missing documents default without blocking, corrupt and unsupported-version documents block before gameplay startup, and unrelated files under the profile root are not deleted. This is not a cloud save system, binary save format, platform user-directory resolver, save migration/backup workflow, filesystem deletion helper, native handle surface, or game-specific save schema.

Runtime Session Profile Resume Plan v1 reviews already loaded profile documents before selected package resume claims: RuntimeSessionProfileResumeRequest carries a RuntimeSessionProfileDocumentLoadResult plus expected save slot, progression checkpoint, selected package id, and persisted profile id; RuntimeSessionProfileResumePlan reports status, loaded/defaulted document counts, save/settings schema versions, and RuntimeSessionProfileResumeDiagnostic rows; and plan_runtime_session_profile_resume fails closed for blocking document rows, missing save.slot/progression.checkpoint/package.id evidence, and mismatched save-slot/progression/package/profile values. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_3d_package expose selected --require-runtime-profile-resume counters including runtime_profile_resume_ready, runtime_profile_resume_loaded_documents, runtime_profile_resume_defaulted_documents, runtime_profile_resume_save_schema_version, and runtime_profile_resume_settings_schema_version. This is not a platform user-directory resolver, cloud save system, binary save format, save migration executor, filesystem mutation helper, native handle surface, or game-specific save schema beyond the reviewed resume evidence keys.

Runtime Simulation Persistence v1 reviews already-loaded RuntimeSessionProfileDocuments before simulation resume claims: RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRequest carries document load evidence, expected save slot/world id, current/minimum schema versions, required entity ids, supported migration steps, and key names; RuntimeSimulationPersistencePlan returns RuntimeSimulationPersistenceStatus, RuntimeSimulationPersistentEntityRow values from entity.<id>.(type|region|state_hash), RuntimeSimulationPersistenceMigrationStep rows, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceDiagnostic rows, and RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRemediationAction recommendations; and plan_runtime_simulation_persistence fails closed for blocking save document rows, corrupt save documents, missing save.slot/world.id/snapshot.id/world.tick evidence, malformed or incomplete entity rows, missing required entities, unsupported schema versions, invalid migration rows, and missing migration-chain steps. Runtime Network Transport Adapter Facade v1 exposes RuntimeNetworkLoopbackExchangeRequest, RuntimeNetworkLoopbackExchangeResult, RuntimeNetworkPacketDesc, RuntimeNetworkReceivedPacketRow, IRuntimeNetworkTransportAdapter, and execute_runtime_network_loopback_exchange for one reviewed loopback-only exchange through caller-owned adapters. MK_runtime_network_enet is an optional network-enet/vcpkg adapter proof over ENet loopback only. This is not external network readiness, socket ownership for generated games, encryption/authentication, matchmaking/NAT traversal, replication, rollback, package mutation, native handle exposure, or broad multiplayer readiness.

Runtime Simulation Orchestration v1 is a host-independent value contract for replay-ready generated gameplay loops: RuntimeSimulationOrchestrationRequest carries simulation_id, next_tick, fixed_tick_rate_hz, accumulated_time_us, max_steps_per_frame, and caller-owned RuntimeSimulationInputCommandDesc rows; plan_runtime_simulation_orchestration returns deterministic fixed-step rows, ordered input-command playback rows, available/planned step counts, consumed/remaining time, and budget_limited status only when diagnostics pass. Diagnostics fail closed for missing simulation ids, invalid fixed tick rates, invalid max steps per frame, missing or duplicate command ids, missing action ids, commands before next_tick, and commands beyond the planned window. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package now exposes selected --require-simulation-orchestration package evidence through simulation_orchestration_* counters for planned fixed-step rows, ordered command playback rows, budget-limited frames, invalid command diagnostics, consumed/remaining time, and clean reviewed-loop diagnostics. This is not a game-rule scheduler, renderer frame pacing system, networking rollback or lockstep layer, package mutation path, editor runtime host, background worker, platform timing API, native handle surface, or broad deterministic multiplayer readiness claim.

Production Authoring Workflows v1 exposes ProductionAuthoringWorkflowRequest, ProductionAuthoringWorkflowRow, ProductionAuthoringWorkflowReviewResult, and review_production_authoring_workflow so reviewed authoring hosts can validate scene placement, quest/dialogue, item/economy, AI behavior, world region, and validation repair workflow rows before game-owned edits or operator validation. The review returns accepted workflow rows, mutation ledger rows, validation repair rows, and diagnostics only; selected sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-production-authoring-workflows smoke emits production_authoring_workflow_* counters for six accepted workflows, six mutation-ledger rows, one validation-repair row, fail-closed shared-surface/arbitrary-shell/cooked-package/native-backend/non-game-target diagnostics, and zero invoked file mutation, package IO, or command execution. This is value-only review metadata, not file mutation, cooked package mutation, arbitrary shell execution, editor action execution, renderer/RHI work, native handle exposure, autonomous content generation, or broad production authoring automation.

Runtime UI Workbench Production v1 exposes RuntimeUiWorkbenchDocument, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPanelRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTableRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchGraphSeries, RuntimeUiWorkbenchItemRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTextInputFieldRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchFocusPlan, RuntimeUiWorkbenchLocalizationRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchAccessibilityRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPlan, and plan_runtime_ui_workbench so generated games can review dense menu, inventory, equipment, shop, simulation dashboard, table, graph, focus, text-input, localization, and accessibility intent rows before adapter work. The planner returns deterministic rows only when diagnostics pass and fail-closed diagnostics cover duplicate ids, empty or colliding graph/item/text/focus ids, unknown panel/table/focus references, invalid graph points, invalid item/equipment/shop/text-input rows, missing localization/accessibility metadata, and bounded backend/native/editor/RHI/UI-middleware tokens. Selected sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package --require-runtime-ui-workbench smoke emits runtime_ui_workbench_* counters for five panels, three table columns, two table rows, two graph series, three item rows, inventory/equipment/shop rows, one text-input row, one platform text-input request, four focus edges, seventeen localization refs, localization identity readiness, eleven accessibility refs, accessibility identity readiness, zero renderer/text-shaping/font-rasterization/IME/accessibility-bridge/image-decoding/native-platform adapter invocations, and clean diagnostics. This is value-only UI intent and package evidence, not renderer submission, text shaping, font rasterization, IME session execution, OS accessibility publication, image decoding, renderer texture upload, native handle exposure, Dear ImGui/SDL3/UI middleware coupling, or broad production text/font/accessibility readiness.

Asset Placeholder Generation v1 exposes plan_placeholder_asset_bundle and plan_placeholder_asset_cook_package through placeholder_asset_tool.hpp so reviewed engine tooling can derive deterministic first-party placeholder texture, mesh, material, and audio source documents, synchronized GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry content, changed-file hashes, provenance rows, and registered source cook/package planning through PlaceholderAssetBundleRequest, PlaceholderAssetBundlePlan, PlaceholderAssetCookPackageRequest, PlaceholderAssetCookPackagePlan, PlaceholderAssetChangedFile, PlaceholderAssetProvenanceRow, and PlaceholderAssetDiagnostic. Selected replacement recooks preserve stable AssetKey rows, source revisions, content hashes, provenance/license rows, existing source registry/package index handoff, and package handoff counters through game.agent.json placeholderAssetPipeline replacementWorkflow rows for sprite, mesh, material, audio, UI, and scene-prop placeholder coverage. Diagnostics fail closed for unsafe registry/source/imported paths, duplicate asset keys, unsupported asset kinds, invalid texture dimensions, and invalid audio/material shapes. This is not an external asset downloader, image/audio generation service, broad source importer execution path, renderer/RHI residency path, package streaming path, game-specific art direction system, native handle surface, or broad generated-game production readiness claim.

Sprite Atlas Authoring v1 exposes SpriteAtlasSourcePivot, SpriteAtlasSourceSliceBorder, SpriteAtlasSourcePagePolicyDesc, SpriteAtlasSourceFrameDesc, SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringDesc, SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringPlan, SpriteAtlasSourceChangedFile, SpriteAtlasSourceFrameRow, SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringDiagnostic, and plan_sprite_atlas_source_authoring through sprite_atlas_tool.hpp so reviewed generated 2D host/tooling workflows can validate already-decoded RGBA8 source frames, canonicalize frames by frame id, enforce a selected single-page-tight-rgba8-texture-source page policy, pack through deterministic-sprite-atlas-rgba8-max-side, emit GameEngine.TextureSource atlas source content, synchronize one GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry texture row, and return changed-file hashes plus frame page id/index, UV, pivot, slice-border, and provenance rows. Diagnostics fail closed for unsafe registry/source/imported paths, unsupported page policy, invalid page id, invalid or duplicate frame ids, unsafe frame source paths, unsupported pixel formats, invalid dimensions, byte-count mismatches, invalid normalized pivots, invalid normalized slice borders, atlas max-side overflow, source-registry conflicts, and unsupported runtime_source_image_decoding, renderer_rhi_residency, package_streaming, animation_semantics, editor_productization, and free_form_edit requests. This is not runtime source image decoding, renderer/RHI residency, package streaming, multi-page atlas paging, padded atlas pages, production sprite batching, animation semantics, editor productization, free-form art editing, or game-specific art direction.

Sprite Batching Renderer v1 exposes SpriteBatchBudgetLane, SpriteBatchBudgetProfileStatus, SpriteBatchBudgetDiagnosticCode, SpriteBatchBudgetDesc, SpriteBatchBudgetLanePlanDesc, SpriteBatchBudgetRow, SpriteBatchBudgetDiagnostic, SpriteBatchBudgetProfile, and plan_sprite_batch_budget_profile so reviewed renderer/gameplay workflows can evaluate world, UI, and effects lane budgets over existing SpriteBatchPlan rows. Diagnostics fail closed for missing plans, invalid budgets, plan diagnostics, sprite budget excess, draw budget excess, and texture-bind budget excess. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package reports sprite_batch_budget_status, sprite_batch_budget_profiles_ready, sprite_batch_budget_rows, sprite_batch_budget_diagnostics, sprite_batch_budget_total_sprites, sprite_batch_budget_total_draws, sprite_batch_budget_total_texture_binds, per-lane ready counters, and per-lane max budget counters alongside existing sprite_batch_plan_* and native_2d_sprite_batch_* package evidence. This is selected plan/budget/native-execution evidence only; material policy, transparency/sorting quality, high-density performance proof, backend renderer-quality gates, Metal readiness, and broad production sprite batching readiness remain follow-up work.

Foundation-only Asset Identity authoring/reference surface is ready for the Engine 1.0 identity boundary: GameEngine.AssetIdentity stable keys, deterministic validation, AssetId derivation, text IO, GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry source registration, plan_asset_identity_placements production placement rows, Reviewed command-owned apply surfaces, Scene runtime package migration placement_rows, ContentBrowserState Asset Identity labels, SourceAssetRegistryDocument population through ContentBrowserState::refresh_from, retained content_browser_import.assets key/source labels, GameEngine.Project project.source_registry, refresh_content_browser_from_project_source_registry, visible MK_editor Reload Source Registry loading, audit_runtime_scene_asset_identity runtime-scene provenance rows, and AssetKey key-first committed game plus tools/new-game.ps1 template references are implemented. Runtime/cooked/render/UI payloads still consume validated AssetId; runtime source registry parsing, renderer/RHI residency, package streaming, native handles, and 2D/3D vertical slices remain outside this identity closeout.

Cooked metadata-only GameEngine.UiAtlas document validation/serialization is implemented in MK_assets, and MK_tools exposes author_cooked_ui_atlas_metadata, write_cooked_ui_atlas_metadata, verify_cooked_ui_atlas_package_metadata, PackedUiAtlasAuthoringDesc, author_packed_ui_atlas_from_decoded_images, plan_packed_ui_atlas_package_update, apply_packed_ui_atlas_package_update, PackedUiGlyphAtlasAuthoringDesc, author_packed_ui_glyph_atlas_from_rasterized_glyphs, plan_packed_ui_glyph_atlas_package_update, and apply_packed_ui_glyph_atlas_package_update for deterministic AssetKind::ui_atlas package artifacts, GameEngine.CookedTexture page artifacts, UiAtlasMetadataImage rows, UiAtlasMetadataGlyph rows, RuntimeUiAtlasGlyph rows, and ui_atlas_texture dependency rows. Runtime source parsing, SVG/vector parsing, renderer texture upload, real font loading/rasterization, text shaping, public native/RHI handles, and broad renderer quality remain unsupported.

Cooked metadata-only GameEngine.Tilemap document validation/serialization is implemented in MK_assets, MK_tools exposes author_cooked_tilemap_metadata, write_cooked_tilemap_metadata, verify_cooked_tilemap_package_metadata, plan_cooked_tilemap_package_update, and apply_cooked_tilemap_package_update for deterministic AssetKind::tilemap package artifacts and tilemap_texture dependency rows, and MK_runtime inspection validates the atlas texture row, path, and dependency edge. Runtime visible-cell sampling and editor package diagnostics are implemented through the later tilemap runtime UX slice; runtime image decoding and production atlas/editor/runtime integration beyond the reviewed source-image decode, atlas-packing tools, and narrow runtime diagnostics, full tilemap editor UX, native GPU sprite batching, package streaming execution, public native/RHI handles, Metal readiness, and renderer quality remain unsupported or host-gated.

packageSurfaces.notes: Default source-tree build/test/sample lane for headless generated games and engine validation.
Manifest-derived config-only package scaffold with NullRenderer fallback.
Manifest-derived cooked scene package scaffold with runtime package load and renderer-neutral scene submission.
Generated material/shader scaffold with D3D12 host-built shader artifacts on supported Windows hosts and Vulkan strict validation only when explicitly gated.
Android package templates and host validation exist on configured hosts, but generated games must run mobile-check and selected Android package commands before claiming readiness.
iOS and Metal readiness requires macOS/Xcode/Apple validation and must remain gated on non-Apple hosts.

physicsBackendAdapterDecisions.decision: Physics 1.0 ready-surface work remains first-party MK_physics only; the optional physics-jolt lane may build MK_physics_jolt, but generated-game defaults and 1.0 ready claims must not require Jolt, native middleware, or public native physics handles.
physicsBackendAdapterDecisions.futureGate: The optional native-physics-middleware-adapter-v1 lane landed through PR #209 / merge commit ccce45701ff3a361977d6286affd46f0ebc16f0d and owns the vcpkg manifest feature physics-jolt, tools/validate-physics-jolt.ps1, docs/dependencies.md, docs/legal-and-licensing.md, THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, first-party facade types, deterministic conversion/replay/budget tests, and fail-closed capability negotiation. Any broader middleware parity, cross-platform determinism claim, generated-game default adoption, or public native-handle interop still requires a new dated plan with benchmark and host evidence.
unsupportedClaims: Jolt runtime integration in the default/generated-game 1.0 ready surface
native physics handles in public gameplay APIs
dependency-free 1.0 ready surface requiring third-party physics

hostGates.notes: D3D12 is the primary currently verified Windows visible/package lane, but gameplay APIs still must not expose D3D12 or RHI handles.
Vulkan package/visible claims require explicit strict smoke commands and local runtime/toolchain readiness. Do not generalize Windows proof to other hosts.
Metal visible presentation and Apple package readiness remain Apple-host gated and must not be marked ready from Windows validation.

hosts: configured signing and AVD/device when required
hostGates.notes: Android Debug/Release/signing/emulator lanes depend on mobile-check output and selected game package commands.

hosts: WIN32 CMake targets MK_editor_game_module_driver_probe and MK_editor_game_module_driver_load_tests
hostGates.notes: Windows-only editor game module driver reload transaction CTest lane requires local MSVC dev preset build outputs for the probe shared library and load-test executable. Does not launch mirakana_editor or claim active-session hot reload or stable third-party ABI.

hosts: default Windows Visual Studio dev preset through CMake File API codemodel
Makefile or Ninja generators with native compile_commands.json
hostGates.notes: Strict clang-tidy compile database availability is ready for the default dev validation path. tools/build.ps1 prepares CMake File API codemodel data before the dev configure/build, tools/validate.ps1 reuses that reply for the check-tidy.ps1 -MaxFiles 1 smoke and then runs test.ps1 -SkipBuild, while standalone tools/check-tidy.ps1 still configures the preset when native compile_commands.json is unavailable. tools/build.ps1 and tools/test.ps1 default to automatic CMake/CTest parallelism through official CMake/CTest --parallel options and accept -Jobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit lower throttle; time-sensitive host-resource tests use CTest RUN_SERIAL rather than disabling suite-wide parallelism. The wrapper synthesizes clang-tidy arguments rows for Visual Studio generator builds, supports -Files for targeted changed-file clang-tidy on compile-database source files, and still reports missing CMake or clang-tidy as local tool blockers.

toolSurfaces.writeScope: Generated-game tasks write game-owned roots by default; shared engine, tooling, schema, docs, rules, skill, and manifest surfaces require a developer-owned capability task.
Generated-game tasks write game-owned roots by default; shared engine, tooling, schema, docs, rules, skill, and manifest surfaces require a developer-owned capability task.
Generated-game tasks write game-owned roots by default; shared engine, tooling, schema, docs, rules, skill, and manifest surfaces require a developer-owned capability task.

gameOwnedWriteScopes: game-local source assets and runtime package files declared by game.agent.json
game-local validation evidence under configured evidence roots
sharedSurfaceWritePolicy: Engine, editor, renderer, tools, schemas, docs, rules, skills, subagents, and manifest fragments require a developer-owned capability task and isolated topic branch.
Default branch direct push is forbidden; publish task-owned changes through a PR.
Do not add broad permission grants for raw shells, package managers, force pushes, destructive cleanup, immediate PR merge, or raw gh pr ready.
Use linked-worktree preparation and guarded post-merge cleanup for isolated production tasks.
forbiddenBroadGrants: force push without explicit branch-owned request
arbitrary shell or raw manifest command evaluation
credential or secret storage in repo
unsupportedClaims: autonomous engine-internal edits from game generation

documentationPolicy.copyingPolicy: Use documentation for guidance; do not copy external code unless the license is explicit and recorded.

sourcePolicy.vcpkgConfigurePolicy: CMake configure must not install, restore, or download vcpkg packages; vcpkg-enabled presets use VCPKG_MANIFEST_INSTALL=OFF and VCPKG_INSTALLED_DIR=${sourceDir}/vcpkg_installed.

gameCodeGuidance.entryPoint: Implement mirakana::GameApp and run it through a runner.
gameCodeGuidance.currentHierarchicalWorldNavigation: Generated larger-map games can use NavigationHierarchicalWorldRegion, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPortalEndpoint, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPortal, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPathRequest, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPathResult, NavigationHierarchicalWorldPortalPathRow, and plan_navigation_hierarchical_world_path from MK_navigation for deterministic value-only region graph routing over caller-reviewed world-region refs, nav-data refs, portal ids, endpoint polygon ids, and scene refs. The planner returns stable region_path, world_region_refs, nav_data_refs, portal_path, portal_rows, total_cost, visited_region_count, status, and fail-closed malformed region/ref/portal diagnostics. This does not bake nav data, stream packages, mutate scenes, run background jobs, own scene/physics integration, introduce middleware, or expose renderer/RHI/native handles.
gameCodeGuidance.currentWorldRegionNavigationRefs: Generated larger-map games can pair MK_navigation hierarchical region paths with MK_runtime RuntimeWorldRegionNavigationRefReviewRequest, RuntimeWorldRegionNavigationRefReviewResult, review_runtime_world_region_navigation_refs, RuntimeWorldRegionNavigationPathCacheEntry, RuntimeWorldRegionNavigationPathCacheReviewRequest, RuntimeWorldRegionNavigationPathCacheReviewResult, and review_runtime_world_region_navigation_path_cache to review route-region package residency rows and retained path-cache readiness before reusing package-backed nav rows. The review helpers fail closed for empty routes, duplicate or missing route regions, not-resident package rows, malformed route/portal counts, stale mount/catalog generations, and unrefreshed RuntimeResidentCatalogCache state. They are value-only and do not read packages, refresh catalogs, execute streaming safe points, bake navigation data, mutate worlds, touch renderer/RHI/native handles, or infer broad open-world readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentAssetPlaceholderGeneration: Reviewed engine/tooling workflows can use mirakana::PlaceholderAssetBundleRequest and mirakana::plan_placeholder_asset_bundle from MK_tools placeholder_asset_tool.hpp when missing generated-game assets need deterministic legal stand-ins. The returned PlaceholderAssetBundlePlan emits first-party texture, mesh, material, and audio source documents plus synchronized GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry text, PlaceholderAssetChangedFile hashes, PlaceholderAssetProvenanceRow provenance, and PlaceholderAssetDiagnostic fail-closed diagnostics. Package-authoring workflows can use mirakana::PlaceholderAssetCookPackageRequest and mirakana::plan_placeholder_asset_cook_package to route those generated source documents through the reviewed registered source cook/package planner before runtime package consumption; generated runtime gameplay should still consume cooked packages and must not download external assets, run arbitrary image/audio generation, bypass registered source cook/package validation, parse source assets at runtime, create renderer/RHI residency, or treat placeholders as game-specific final art.
gameCodeGuidance.currentSpriteAtlasSourceAuthoring: Reviewed generated 2D host/tooling workflows can use mirakana::SpriteAtlasSourcePivot, mirakana::SpriteAtlasSourceSliceBorder, mirakana::SpriteAtlasSourcePagePolicyDesc, mirakana::SpriteAtlasSourceFrameDesc, mirakana::SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringDesc, mirakana::SpriteAtlasSourceAuthoringPlan, and mirakana::plan_sprite_atlas_source_authoring from MK_tools sprite_atlas_tool.hpp when already-decoded RGBA8 source frames need deterministic atlas source authoring. The planner validates safe source registry, atlas source/imported, a selected single-page-tight-rgba8-texture-source page policy, frame id, frame source path, RGBA8 pixel-format, byte-count, normalized pivot, normalized slice-border, and max-side rows; canonicalizes frames by frame id; packs through deterministic-sprite-atlas-rgba8-max-side; emits GameEngine.TextureSource atlas source content, synchronized GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry texture registration, SpriteAtlasSourceChangedFile hashes, and SpriteAtlasSourceFrameRow page id/index, UV, pivot, slice-border, and provenance rows only when diagnostics pass. DesktopRuntime2DPackage scaffolds and sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package declare spriteAtlasSourceAuthoringTargets that point at source/assets/package.geassets plus source/sprites/player_atlas.texture_source while keeping both source authoring files out of runtimePackageFiles before cooked runtime consumption. Route the changed files through registered source cook/package validation before runtime package consumption; generated runtime gameplay must not parse PNG/source images, create renderer/RHI residency from source images, stream packages, infer animation semantics, expose editor productization, run free-form edits, or treat atlas source authoring as game-specific art direction.
gameCodeGuidance.currentSpriteBatchBudgetProfile: Generated 2D runtime/package code can use mirakana::plan_sprite_batches and mirakana::plan_sprite_batch_budget_profile from MK_renderer sprite_batch.hpp to evaluate selected world, UI, and effects lane budgets over existing SpriteBatchPlan rows. SpriteBatchBudgetLanePlanDesc carries the lane, plan pointer, and SpriteBatchBudgetDesc maxima; SpriteBatchBudgetProfile reports status, per-lane rows, diagnostics, and total sprite/draw/texture-bind counts with fail-closed diagnostics for missing plans, invalid budgets, plan diagnostics, and sprite/draw/texture-bind budget excess. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package reports sprite_batch_budget_* counters alongside sprite_batch_plan_* and native_2d_sprite_batch_* evidence. This is budget/profile evidence and selected native execution telemetry; generated gameplay must not claim material policy, transparency/sorting quality, high-density performance proof, renderer-quality gates, Metal readiness, native handle access, or broad production sprite batching readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentSpriteAnimationFlipbook: Sprite Animation Flipbook v1 Phase 3 exposes mirakana::RuntimeSpriteFlipbookClipDesc, mirakana::RuntimeSpriteFlipbookDesc, mirakana::RuntimeSpriteFlipbookState, mirakana::RuntimeSpriteFlipbookSampleResult, mirakana::RuntimeSpriteFlipbookDirectionSetRow, mirakana::RuntimeSpriteFlipbookPlaybackRequest, mirakana::RuntimeSpriteFlipbookEventDesc, mirakana::advance_runtime_sprite_flipbook, and mirakana::advance_runtime_sprite_flipbook_playback from MK_scene_renderer so generated 2D gameplay can advance named clips or select clips by gameplay state and direction over cooked mirakana::runtime::RuntimeSpriteAnimationFrame rows with deterministic loop/clamp playback policy, selected-frame evidence, frame-local event rows, and fail-closed diagnostics before applying the sampled frame to scene sprite rows. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package now reports package-visible flipbook counters through --require-sprite-animation, including sprite_flipbook_ticks, sprite_flipbook_frames_sampled, sprite_flipbook_frames_applied, sprite_flipbook_selected_frame_sum, sprite_flipbook_diagnostics, sprite_flipbook_direction_sets, sprite_flipbook_event_rows, sprite_flipbook_playback_modes, sprite_flipbook_gameplay_state_rows, sprite_flipbook_events_sampled, and sprite_flipbook_playback_diagnostics. This is selected value-only state/update and package evidence; game-specific animation graphs and state machines stay in game code, and runtime source image decoding, renderer/RHI residency from source images, editor animation tooling, native handles, and broad production sprite animation tooling remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.currentGameplayDebugOverlay: Generated games and samples can use mirakana::ui::RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayRowDesc plus mirakana::ui::plan_runtime_gameplay_debug_overlay from MK_ui when they need deterministic gameplay debug overlay rows for reviewed gameplay binding, interaction, input, audio, physics, navigation, AI, package, session, or custom status values. RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayCategory, RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayRowKind, RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayDiagnosticCode, RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayRow, RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayDiagnostic, and RuntimeGameplayDebugOverlayPlan keep the contract value-only and fail closed for missing or duplicate row ids, missing labels, unsupported categories, and unsupported row kinds. sample_2d_playable_foundation shows source-tree adoption by reporting debug_overlay_rows=12 from gameplay/input/audio/session rows. This does not render UI, execute debug commands, expose Dear ImGui/editor/SDL3/native handles, create renderer/RHI residency, stream packages, or define game-specific debug schemas.
gameCodeGuidance.currentRuntimeUiWorkbench: Generated games and samples can use mirakana::ui::RuntimeUiWorkbenchDocument, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPanelRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTableRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchGraphSeries, RuntimeUiWorkbenchItemRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchTextInputFieldRow, RuntimeUiWorkbenchFocusPlan, RuntimeUiWorkbenchLocalizationRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchAccessibilityRef, RuntimeUiWorkbenchPlan, and mirakana::ui::plan_runtime_ui_workbench from MK_ui when menu, inventory, equipment, shop, or simulation dashboard UI needs dense retained value rows before renderer or platform adapters run. The planner returns deterministic panel, table, graph, item, text-input, platform_text_input_requests, focus edge, localization, and accessibility rows only when diagnostics are clean, and fails closed for duplicate ids, empty or colliding graph/item/text/focus ids, unknown panel/table/focus references, invalid graph points, invalid item/equipment/shop/text-input rows, missing localization/accessibility metadata, and bounded native/backend/editor/RHI/UI-middleware tokens. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package proves selected package-visible evidence through --require-runtime-ui-workbench with runtime_ui_workbench_status=ready, runtime_ui_workbench_panels=5, runtime_ui_workbench_table_columns=3, runtime_ui_workbench_table_rows=2, runtime_ui_workbench_graph_series=2, runtime_ui_workbench_item_rows=3, runtime_ui_workbench_inventory_rows=1, runtime_ui_workbench_equipment_rows=1, runtime_ui_workbench_shop_rows=1, runtime_ui_workbench_text_inputs=1, runtime_ui_workbench_platform_text_input_requests=1, runtime_ui_workbench_focus_edges=4, runtime_ui_workbench_localization_refs=17, runtime_ui_workbench_localization_identity_ready=1, runtime_ui_workbench_accessibility_refs=11, runtime_ui_workbench_accessibility_identity_ready=1, zero renderer/text-shaping/font-rasterization/IME/accessibility-bridge/image-decoding/native-platform adapter invocation, and runtime_ui_workbench_diagnostics=0. This is value-only runtime UI intent and package evidence; it does not render UI, shape text, rasterize fonts, start IME sessions, publish OS accessibility, decode images, upload renderer textures, expose native handles, depend on Dear ImGui/SDL3/UI middleware/editor APIs, or claim broad production text/font/accessibility readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentGameplayInteractionFramework: Generated games and samples can use mirakana::runtime::RuntimeGameplayInteractionEvent, RuntimeGameplayInteractionState, RuntimeGameplayInteractionPlan, and plan_runtime_gameplay_interactions from MK_runtime for `engine-gameplay-interaction-framework-v1` when trigger, damage, heal, pickup, objective, feedback, win, loss, or restart events need deterministic caller-owned gameplay state rows before game-owned execution. This is backend-neutral value-only package evidence for Lighting Shadow Policy v1; it does not render additional lights, mutate packages, expose native/RHI handles, or claim Vulkan/Metal visual parity or production shadow quality.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dDebugProfilingPolicyPack: sample_desktop_runtime_game can require --require-debug-profiling-policy on package/source-tree smoke runs that already request a cooked scene package and scene GPU bindings. The selected D3D12 package smoke can additionally require --require-d3d12-debug-profiling-evidence, and the separate installed-vulkan-scene-gpu-smoke recipe can require --require-vulkan-debug-profiling-evidence without treating D3D12 proof as cross-backend evidence. The sample evaluates SdlDesktopPresentationDebugProfilingPolicyDesc through evaluate_sdl_desktop_presentation_debug_profiling_policy and reports debug_profiling_policy_status=ready, debug_profiling_policy_ready=1, debug_profiling_policy_diagnostics=0, debug_profiling_policy_scene_resources_ready=1, debug_profiling_policy_frames_current=1, positive debug_profiling_policy_requests, positive debug_profiling_policy_gpu_debug_markers_inserted, debug_profiling_policy_backend_profiling_evidence_required=1, and debug_profiling_policy_backend_profiling_evidence_ready=1 when backend profiling evidence is requested. D3D12 runs additionally report positive debug_profiling_policy_gpu_timestamp_ticks_per_second plus d3d12_debug_profiling_execution_status=ready, d3d12_debug_profiling_execution_ready=1, d3d12_debug_profiling_execution_selected=1, positive d3d12_debug_profiling_execution_gpu_timestamp_ticks_per_second, d3d12_debug_profiling_execution_gpu_timestamps_ok=1, d3d12_debug_profiling_execution_gpu_debug_markers_ok=1, and d3d12_debug_profiling_execution_frame_diagnostics_ok=1. Vulkan runs report vulkan_debug_profiling_execution_status=ready, vulkan_debug_profiling_execution_ready=1, vulkan_debug_profiling_execution_selected=1, positive vulkan_debug_profiling_execution_gpu_debug_markers_inserted, vulkan_debug_profiling_execution_gpu_debug_markers_ok=1, and vulkan_debug_profiling_execution_frame_diagnostics_ok=1 while keeping GPU timestamp ticks host-gated at zero. This is package-visible Debug Profiling Policy v1 evidence plus selected per-backend execution counters; it does not add automatic external capture execution, production flame graphs, crash telemetry export, Metal parity, public native/RHI handles, or package mutation.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dGpuMemoryPolicyPackageSm: sample_desktop_runtime_game can require --require-gpu-memory-policy on package/source-tree smoke runs that already request a cooked scene package and scene GPU bindings. The selected D3D12 package smoke can additionally require --require-d3d12-gpu-memory-evidence, and the separate installed-vulkan-scene-gpu-smoke recipe can require --require-vulkan-gpu-memory-evidence without treating D3D12 proof as cross-backend evidence. The sample evaluates SdlDesktopPresentationGpuMemoryPolicyDesc through evaluate_sdl_desktop_presentation_gpu_memory_policy and reports gpu_memory_policy_status=ready, gpu_memory_policy_ready=1, gpu_memory_policy_diagnostics=0, gpu_memory_policy_scene_resources_ready=1, gpu_memory_policy_frames_current=1, positive gpu_memory_policy_requests, positive gpu_memory_policy_requested_bytes, positive gpu_memory_policy_committed_byte_estimate, positive gpu_memory_policy_upload_bytes_written, gpu_memory_policy_backend_memory_evidence_required=1, and gpu_memory_policy_backend_memory_evidence_ready=1 when backend memory evidence is requested. D3D12 runs additionally require gpu_memory_policy_os_video_memory_budget_required=1 and gpu_memory_policy_os_video_memory_budget_available=1 plus d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_status=ready, d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_ready=1, d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_selected=1, d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_os_video_memory_budget_available=1, d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_committed_byte_estimate_available=1, positive d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_local_video_memory_budget_bytes, positive d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_committed_resources_byte_estimate, positive d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_transient_heap_allocations, positive d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_upload_bytes_written, d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_budget_ok=1, and d3d12_gpu_memory_execution_transient_heap_ok=1. Vulkan runs report vulkan_gpu_memory_execution_status=ready, vulkan_gpu_memory_execution_ready=1, vulkan_gpu_memory_execution_selected=1, vulkan_gpu_memory_execution_committed_byte_estimate_available=1, positive vulkan_gpu_memory_execution_committed_resources_byte_estimate, positive vulkan_gpu_memory_execution_upload_bytes_written, positive vulkan_gpu_memory_execution_framegraph_barrier_steps_executed, vulkan_gpu_memory_execution_budget_ok=1, and vulkan_gpu_memory_execution_transient_heap_ok=1 while keeping OS video-memory budget host-gated off Vulkan. This is package-visible GPU Memory Policy v1 evidence plus selected per-backend execution counters; it does not add automatic eviction, background streaming, performance claims, Metal parity, public native/RHI handles, or package mutation.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dSceneScalePolicyPackageS: sample_desktop_runtime_game can require --require-scene-scale-policy on package/source-tree smoke runs that already request a cooked scene package and scene GPU bindings, and the selected D3D12 package smoke can additionally require --require-d3d12-instanced-draw-evidence, and installed-vulkan-scene-gpu-smoke may require --require-vulkan-instanced-draw-evidence. The sample evaluates SdlDesktopPresentationSceneScalePolicyDesc through evaluate_sdl_desktop_presentation_scene_scale_policy and reports scene_scale_policy_status=ready, scene_scale_policy_ready=1, scene_scale_policy_diagnostics=0, scene_scale_policy_scene_resources_ready=1, scene_scale_policy_frames_current=1, positive scene_scale_policy_draw_groups, positive scene_scale_policy_requested_instances, positive scene_scale_policy_visible_instances, positive scene_scale_policy_draw_calls, scene_scale_policy_culled_instances=0, scene_scale_policy_instanced_draw_calls=0, scene_scale_policy_lod_groups=0, scene_scale_policy_cpu_culling_groups=0, scene_scale_policy_backend_instancing_evidence_required=1, and scene_scale_policy_backend_instancing_evidence_ready=1 when D3D12 instanced evidence is requested. It also evaluates SdlDesktopPresentationD3d12InstancedDrawExecutionReport through evaluate_sdl_desktop_presentation_d3d12_instanced_draw_execution and reports d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_status=ready, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_ready=1, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_selected=1, d3d12_instanced_draw_execution_expected_instances=6, positive d3d12_instanced_draw_calls, positive d3d12_instanced_indexed_draw_calls, positive d3d12_instanced_instances_submitted, positive rhi_instanced_draw_calls, positive rhi_instanced_indexed_draw_calls, positive rhi_instanced_instances_submitted, d3d12_instanced_draws_ok=1, and d3d12_instanced_instances_ok=1, vulkan_instanced_draw_execution_status=ready, vulkan_instanced_draw_execution_ready=1, vulkan_instanced_draw_execution_selected=1, positive vulkan_instanced_draw_calls, positive vulkan_instanced_indexed_draw_calls, positive vulkan_instanced_instances_submitted, vulkan_instanced_draws_ok=1, vulkan_instanced_instances_ok=1. This is package-visible Scene Scale Policy v1 draw/instance evidence plus selected D3D12 instanced draw execution evidence; it does not add GPU indirect draws, GPU culling, performance claims, Vulkan/Metal parity, public native/RHI handles, or package mutation.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dShadowMorphCompositionPa: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-shadow-morph-composition on a selected installed D3D12 package smoke that requests scene GPU bindings, morph package data, depth-aware postprocess, renderer quality gates, and fixed PCF directional shadow filtering without compute morph, skin composition, or playable aggregate flags. The generated 3D D3D12/Vulkan scene renderer descriptors keep graphics morph vertex shader and SdlDesktopPresentationSceneMorphMeshBinding rows active, compile shifted shadow receiver artifacts named shadow_receiver_shifted.ps.dxil and shadow_receiver_shifted.ps.spv with MK_SAMPLE_SHIFTED_SCENE_SHADOW_RECEIVER_PS, bind the shadow receiver descriptor set after the deformation descriptor set, and report scene_gpu_morph_mesh_bindings, scene_gpu_morph_mesh_uploads, scene_gpu_morph_mesh_resolved, scene_gpu_uploaded_morph_bytes, renderer_gpu_morph_draws, renderer_morph_descriptor_binds, directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, fixed_pcf_3x3 filtering, framegraph_passes=3, framegraph_passes_executed=6, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=6, and framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=15 through first-party status fields. This is a narrow generated 3D graphics morph + directional shadow receiver package smoke; morph-deformed shadow-caster silhouettes, compute morph + shadow composition, graphics morph+skin composition, hardware comparison samplers, cascades, atlases, production shadow quality, Metal shadow presentation, GPU timestamps, backend-native stats, native handles, and broad renderer quality remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dNativeUiOverlayPackageSm: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-native-ui-overlay on a selected installed D3D12 package smoke that already requests scene GPU bindings, postprocess, renderer quality gates, playable_3d aggregate evidence, gameplay systems, and generated UI overlay DXIL shader artifacts. The generated 3D game builds a first-party MK_ui HUD box, submits it through MK_ui_renderer into the host-owned native UI overlay path, enables SdlDesktopPresentationD3d12SceneRendererDesc::enable_native_ui_overlay without texture atlas mode, and reports hud_boxes=2, ui_overlay_requested=1, ui_overlay_status=ready, ui_overlay_ready=1, ui_overlay_sprites_submitted=2, and ui_overlay_draws=2 through first-party status fields. This is a narrow generated 3D HUD box native UI overlay smoke; textured image-sprite atlas behavior is covered by desktopRuntime3dNativeUiTexturedSpriteAtlasPackageSmoke and text glyph atlas behavior is covered by desktopRuntime3dNativeUiTextGlyphAtlasPackageSmoke, while production text shaping, font rasterization, glyph atlas generation, runtime source image decoding, OS accessibility bridges, Metal overlay readiness, native handles, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dEntityScaleCullingPackag: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-entity-scale-culling on a lightweight installed package smoke that already requests a cooked config and .geindex scene package. The smoke calls mirakana::runtime::plan_runtime_entity_scale_culling with deterministic 2D/3D entity rows and reports entity_scale_culling_status=planned, entity_scale_culling_ready=1, entity_scale_culling_rows=4, entity_scale_culling_visible_rows=2, entity_scale_culling_culled_rows=2, entity_scale_culling_lod_rows=2, entity_scale_culling_priority_update_rows=1, entity_scale_culling_normal_update_rows=2, entity_scale_culling_background_update_rows=1, positive entity_scale_culling_projected_draw_cost, positive entity_scale_culling_projected_update_cost, entity_scale_culling_budget_protected_rows=1, entity_scale_culling_diagnostics=0, and entity_scale_culling_budget_diagnostics=2 through first-party status fields without D3D12/Vulkan renderer selection, scene GPU bindings, or gameplay systems. This extends Entity Scale Culling Package Evidence v1 to generated 3D; it does not mutate scene nodes, perform GPU culling, expose native/RHI handles, or prove broad high-object-count performance.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dVisibleProductionPackage: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-visible-3d-production-proof on a selected installed D3D12 package smoke or --require-vulkan-visible-3d-production-proof on desktop-runtime-release-target-vulkan-visible-production-proof-toolchain-gated without treating either backend proof as cross-backend evidence. Both paths require scene GPU bindings, postprocess, renderer quality gates, playable_3d aggregate evidence, gameplay systems, native UI overlay, and target-specific UI overlay shader artifacts. The proof aggregates visible_3d_status=ready, visible_3d_ready=1, visible_3d_diagnostics=0, visible_3d_expected_frames=2, visible_3d_presented_frames=2, visible_3d_d3d12_selected=1 or visible_3d_vulkan_selected=1, visible_3d_null_fallback_used=0, visible_3d_scene_gpu_ready=1, visible_3d_postprocess_ready=1, visible_3d_renderer_quality_ready=1, visible_3d_playable_ready=1, and visible_3d_ui_overlay_ready=1 through first-party status fields. This is a narrow per-backend visible production-style aggregate; it is not Metal parity, native/RHI handle exposure, broad renderer quality, broad runtime UI readiness, or broad generated 3D production readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dNativeUiTexturedSpriteAt: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-native-ui-textured-sprite-atlas on a selected installed D3D12 package smoke that already requests scene GPU bindings, postprocess, renderer quality gates, playable_3d aggregate evidence, gameplay systems, generated UI overlay artifacts, and a cooked runtime GameEngine.UiAtlas asset. The flag implies --require-native-ui-overlay behavior while additionally exercising image-sprite atlas submission through MK_ui_renderer and reporting hud_images=2, ui_atlas_metadata_status=ready, ui_atlas_metadata_pages=1, ui_atlas_metadata_bindings=1, ui_texture_overlay_status=ready, ui_texture_overlay_atlas_ready=1, ui_texture_overlay_sprites_submitted=2, ui_texture_overlay_texture_binds=2, and ui_texture_overlay_draws=2 alongside existing hud_boxes and ui_overlay_* counters. This is a narrow generated 3D texture-backed UI atlas smoke; production text shaping, font rasterization, glyph atlas generation, runtime source image decoding, source image atlas packing, Metal parity, native handles, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dNativeUiTextGlyphAtlasPa: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-native-ui-text-glyph-atlas on a selected installed D3D12 package smoke that already requests scene GPU bindings, postprocess, renderer quality gates, playable_3d aggregate evidence, gameplay systems, generated UI overlay artifacts, and a cooked runtime GameEngine.UiAtlas text glyph metadata asset. The flag implies --require-native-ui-overlay behavior while exercising one deterministic engine-default glyph through MK_ui_renderer and reporting hud_text_glyphs=2, text_glyphs_available=2, text_glyphs_resolved=2, text_glyphs_missing=0, text_glyph_sprites_submitted=2, ui_atlas_metadata_status=ready, ui_atlas_metadata_pages=1, ui_atlas_metadata_bindings=0, ui_atlas_metadata_glyphs=1, ui_texture_overlay_status=ready, ui_texture_overlay_atlas_ready=1, ui_texture_overlay_sprites_submitted=2, ui_texture_overlay_texture_binds=2, and ui_texture_overlay_draws=2 alongside existing hud_boxes and ui_overlay_* counters. This is a narrow generated 3D cooked glyph metadata smoke; production text shaping, font rasterization, glyph atlas generation, runtime source image decoding, source image atlas packing, Metal parity, native handles, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dPlayablePackageSmoke: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-playable-3d-slice on selected installed package smokes that already request package streaming, scene GPU bindings, postprocess, and renderer quality gates. The smoke reports playable_3d_status, playable_3d_ready, playable_3d_diagnostics, playable_3d_expected_frames, playable_3d_scene_mesh_plan_ready, playable_3d_camera_controller_ready, playable_3d_animation_ready, playable_3d_morph_ready, playable_3d_quaternion_ready, playable_3d_package_streaming_ready, playable_3d_scene_gpu_ready, playable_3d_postprocess_ready, playable_3d_renderer_quality_ready, and selected compute readiness fields through first-party status output. This is a narrow generated 3D playable package smoke; runtime source import, broad dependency cooking, broad/background package streaming, Metal parity, native handles, general renderer quality, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.runtimeMorphMeshGpuBinding: Narrow D3D12 primary-lane GPU morph proof: MK_runtime_rhi exposes upload_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu, make_runtime_morph_mesh_gpu_binding, and attach_morph_mesh_descriptor_set for RuntimeMorphMeshCpuPayload POSITION deltas at morph descriptor binding 0, optional NORMAL/TANGENT deltas at bindings 2/3, and 256-byte weight uniform binding 1; MK_renderer exposes MorphMeshGpuBinding normal_delta_buffer/tangent_delta_buffer metadata, MeshCommand::gpu_morphing, RhiFrameRendererDesc::morph_graphics_pipeline, gpu_morph_draws, and morph_descriptor_binds. Direct D3D12 readback proves graphics-pipeline POSITION deformation and NORMAL/TANGENT lit-output changes; RuntimeMorphMeshComputeBinding and create_runtime_morph_mesh_compute_binding now prove direct D3D12 compute POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT output readback, make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding proves POSITION renderer consumption, generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage D3D12/Vulkan smoke proves POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT compute output rendering plus package-visible queue-wait evidence when explicitly requested, make_runtime_compute_morph_skinned_mesh_gpu_binding plus SkinnedMeshGpuBinding::skin_attribute_vertex_buffer prove a D3D12 runtime/renderer skin+compute-morph composition path with a distinct joint-palette descriptor set, MK_runtime_scene_rhi retains RuntimeSceneComputeMorphSkinnedMeshBinding rows in SceneSkinnedGpuBindingPalette through a host-owned runtime scene RHI bridge, and generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage D3D12/Vulkan smoke can now require first-party skin+compute package counters while D3D12 also reports scene_gpu_compute_morph_async_* sequencing telemetry from IRhiDevice::stats(). RHI/D3D12 queue-submit and queue-wait fence sequencing telemetry is covered by runtime-rhi-compute-morph-async-telemetry-d3d12-v1 and generated package promotion is covered by generated-3d-compute-morph-async-telemetry-package-smoke-d3d12-v1. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Async Overlap Evidence D3D12 v1 adds mirakana::rhi::RhiAsyncOverlapReadinessDiagnostics and diagnose_compute_graphics_async_overlap_readiness, which deterministically reports not_proven_serial_dependency for the current same-frame graphics queue wait without exposing native handles, timestamp resources, or package-visible performance claims. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Pipelined Output Ring D3D12 v1 adds RuntimeMorphMeshComputeBindingOptions::output_slot_count, RuntimeMorphMeshComputeOutputSlot, slot-indexed make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding, and D3D12 descriptor-range proof that slot 1 can receive compute-written POSITION output through binding 3 without aliasing slot 0. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Pipelined Scheduling D3D12 v1 adds mirakana::rhi::RhiPipelinedComputeGraphicsScheduleEvidence and diagnose_pipelined_compute_graphics_async_overlap_readiness so first-party diagnostics can classify graphics consuming a previous output slot while compute writes a different current slot as backend-private timing-ready rather than a same-frame serial dependency. RHI D3D12 Per-Queue Fence Synchronization v1 completed first-party FenceValue queue identity and backend-private per-queue D3D12 fence timelines. Vulkan async/performance work, Metal parity, graphics morph+skin composition, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain follow-up work; rhi-d3d12-queue-timestamp-measurement-foundation-v1 is the active RHI timing slice for backend-private graphics/compute timestamp interval diagnostics.
gameCodeGuidance.runtimeSceneRhiMorphGpuPalette: Host-owned package bridge for the GPU morph proof: MK_runtime_scene_rhi accepts RuntimeSceneGpuBindingOptions::morph_mesh_assets, validates selected cooked morph_mesh_cpu records, uploads them through MK_runtime_rhi including optional NORMAL/TANGENT delta streams when present, retains SceneMorphGpuBindingPalette plus morph_descriptor_set_layout and morph_mesh_uploads, reports morph_mesh_uploads/uploaded_morph_bytes, and releases morph buffers/descriptors through execute_runtime_scene_gpu_binding_safe_point_teardown on NullRhiDevice. Generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds can request/report host-owned palette counters, generated-package POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT visible graphics morph smoke is tracked separately, and generated-package POSITION compute morph smoke is tracked by desktopRuntime3dComputeMorphPackageSmoke; the palette bridge itself does not add scene schema morph bindings, compute dispatch ownership, Vulkan/Metal parity, or gameplay-visible RHI handles.

gameCodeGuidance.currentRhi: BackendCapabilityProfile, BackendProbePlan, and BackendProbeResult contracts with host support policy, preferred backend order, shader artifact formats, and deterministic D3D12/Vulkan/Metal bootstrap steps, plus RhiResourceLifetimeRegistry resource ids, owner labels, debug names, generation-checked handles, deferred-release records, queue-qualified fence retirement, diagnostics, marker-style lifetime events, NullRhiDevice lifetime wiring, and D3D12/Vulkan deferred native teardown for committed resources and pipeline-class objects without exposing native handles or claiming GPU allocator enforcement. Metal IRhiDevice parity and Apple-host evidence remain separate host-gated work. SDK-independent MK_rhi_vulkan/MK_rhi_metal backend scaffold targets, plus NullRhiDevice contract with resources, samplers, swapchains, explicit swapchain frame acquire/release, backend-neutral swapchain resize, shaders, descriptor set layouts, descriptor sets, upload-buffer writes, copy/upload/readback commands with explicit texture footprints, CPU buffer readback, transient resource leases, presentation ownership and sequencing, single-pending-frame swapchain throttling, graphics pipelines with vertex input layouts/attributes plus DepthStencilStateDesc depth-test/write/compare state, render passes with explicit clear colors plus optional RenderPassDepthAttachment and ClearDepthValue, vertex/index buffer binding, direct/indexed draw calls, command lists, resource transitions, fence semantics, and backend-neutral write/copy/read/fence/sampler stats, GPU debug-scope/marker stats, D3D12 graphics-queue timestamp frequency reporting, and read-only memory_diagnostics snapshots. RHI Depth Attachment Contract v0 is backend-neutral and NullRHI-validated; Depth Sampling Contract v0 allows depth24_stencil8 textures to use depth_stencil|shader_resource with explicit depth_write-to-shader_read transitions while keeping native views private; D3D12 now implements private DSV-backed depth attachments, sampled depth textures through typeless DSV/SRV views, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth validation, readback-observable depth-test proof, sampled-depth readback proof, depth-aware postprocess readback proof, and directional shadow receiver readback proof plus fixed sampled-depth 3x3 PCF boundary readback proof through the public RHI contract; Vulkan now implements private depth-aspect image views, D24S8 depth|stencil barrier aspect masks, required synchronization2 feature query/device enablement, depth+sampled create planning, sampled-depth descriptor update coverage, depth_write synchronization2 barriers, depth_write-to-shader_read shader-read barriers, dynamic-rendering depth attachments, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth validation, texture-state compatibility checks, environment-gated depth-ordered readback proof, environment-gated visible sampled-depth readback proof, environment-gated postprocess-depth readback proof, and environment-gated shadow receiver readback proof through the same public RHI contract; Metal native depth recording/sampling, Metal postprocess depth, Metal shadow presentation, broader postprocess effects, and production shadow-map quality remain follow-up work
gameCodeGuidance.currentD3d12: Windows-only MK_rhi_d3d12 scaffold validates D3D12/DXGI header integration, links D3D12/DXGI libraries, probes DXGI factory plus hardware/WARP D3D12 support, validates minimal device, direct command queue, fence bootstrap, creates first committed upload/readback/default texture resources, and exposes a PIMPL DeviceContext for persistent D3D12 device/resource/command list ownership with close/reset/execute, fence signaling, completed-fence queries, CPU-side fence waits, GPU-side cross-queue waits, HWND swapchain creation, present, resize, RTV/DSV ownership, back-buffer transitions, standalone texture transitions, requested-color swapchain and texture render-target clears, requested-depth DSV clears, shader-visible descriptor heap ownership/binding, native CBV/SRV/UAV descriptor view writes, native sampler descriptor writes, root signature ownership/binding, graphics descriptor table binding with sampler table splitting, shader bytecode ownership, graphics PSO ownership/binding with optional input layouts and native depth-stencil state, swapchain and texture render-target setup, private depth-stencil view setup, upload-buffer writes, buffer and aligned buffer/texture copy recording, readback buffer mapping, shared committed texture creation for TextureUsage::shared, first-party D3D12 shared texture export handles, tracked texture state validation before render/copy/transition commands, stale cross-queue texture-state submission rejection, descriptor/root-signature/PSO validation failure proof, non-black render-target clear readback proof, renderer viewport clear-color readback proof, first-triangle texture readback proof, depth-tested overlapping-triangle texture readback proof, shared texture export proof, swapchain present sequencing proof, swapchain resize proof, explicit swapchain frame acquire/release proof, single-pending-frame throttling proof, backend-neutral fence lifecycle stats, shared texture export/failure stats, and first triangle draw recording. It also exposes an initial IRhiDevice bridge for backend-neutral buffers, textures including depth24_stencil8 depth_stencil and depth_stencil|shader_resource textures, sampler handle validation, upload-buffer writes, descriptor set layout/allocation/update validation backed by separate CBV/SRV/UAV and sampler shader-visible descriptor arenas and native descriptor writes, shader bytecode, pipeline layouts, graphics pipelines with vertex input layouts/attributes plus DepthStencilStateDesc mapping, buffer copies, aligned buffer/texture copies, CPU buffer readback, standalone texture transition commands, committed-resource-backed transient leases, surface-backed swapchains, explicit swapchain frame acquire/release, backend-neutral swapchain resize, graphics-queue-only queued presentation, render-pass-complete-before-present sequencing, duplicate-present rejection, single-pending-frame throttling until release or present/submit, swapchain and texture render passes with optional private DSV depth attachments, descriptor set binding across split CBV/SRV/UAV and sampler root tables, vertex/index buffer binding, direct/indexed draw recording, command-list submission, fence waits, fence wait/failure/last-submitted/last-completed stats, shared texture exports, and renderer-facing frame submission/display through RhiFrameRenderer and RhiViewportSurface. Native ID3D12Object::SetName labels are applied to the device, graphics/compute/copy queues, submission fence, committed buffers and textures, swapchain back buffers and swapchain RTV descriptor heaps, CBV/SRV/UAV and sampler descriptor heaps, root signatures, graphics pipeline states, per-command-list allocators and command lists, and bootstrap proof upload/readback/default resources for PIX and GPU-based validation object listings. Native D3D12 sampler descriptors are implemented with a separate shader-visible sampler arena, root-signature table splitting by heap kind, descriptor-set binding across CBV/SRV/UAV plus sampler root tables, a visible sampled-texture-plus-sampler readback proof, a D3D12-visible RhiFrameRenderer runtime material texture-sampling proof, a D3D12-visible uploaded runtime mesh vertex/index buffer readback proof, a D3D12-visible cooked runtime scene GPU palette indexed draw/readback proof, and an installed SDL3 sample_desktop_runtime_game proof that renders a packaged cooked scene through host-owned D3D12 scene GPU binding injection using target-specific scene DXIL artifacts. Installed SDL3 D3D12 package-visible postprocess depth and fixed sampled-depth 3x3 PCF directional shadow filtering are validated above the backend through private DXIL shader artifacts; hardware comparison samplers, production shadow quality, and Metal depth remain follow-up work. Sampled depth textures are implemented through private typeless resources, D24 DSV views, R24 SRV views, explicit depth_write-to-shader_read transition validation, sampled descriptor writes, D3D12 sampled-depth readback proof, D3D12 depth-aware postprocess readback proof, D3D12 directional shadow receiver readback proof, and D3D12 fixed sampled-depth 3x3 PCF boundary readback proof.

for platform Vulkan runtime and vkGetInstanceProcAddr availability, probe_runtime_global_commands for real loader/global command readiness through vkGetInstanceProcAddr(nullptr, ...), vkGetDeviceQueue, vkDeviceWaitIdle, vkDestroyDevice, and surface format/present-mode command handling, create_runtime_command_pool for move-only VulkanRuntimeCommandPool ownership of a private command pool and primary command buffer with begin/end recording calls, create_runtime_buffer, write_runtime_buffer, read_runtime_buffer, and create_runtime_texture for private buffer/memory and image/memory resource ownership including depth-aspect image views for sampled descriptors, D24S8 depth|stencil image-barrier aspect masks, depth+sampled create planning, and upload/readback-domain CPU mapping, create_rhi_device for a mapping-plan-gated backend-neutral IRhiDevice bridge that creates buffers, textures, sampler handles, writes upload buffers, creates transient leases, shader modules, descriptor set layouts/sets, descriptor updates including sampled texture and sampler writes, pipeline layouts, graphics pipelines with vertex input layouts/attributes plus DepthStencilStateDesc depth mapping, surface-backed swapchains from actual surface capabilities/formats/present modes, explicit swapchain frame acquire/release, swapchain resize, graphics command lists with command-list-local texture state snapshots, shader-free swapchain clear render passes, standalone texture clears/draws/copies with optional private depth attachments, descriptor set binding that must match the currently bound pipeline layout, vertex/index buffer binding, indexed draw recording, stale texture-state submit rejection, submit-success-only texture-state commits, queued present on submit, copy-only submit, vkCreateShaderModule/vkDestroyShaderModule calls, create_runtime_descriptor_set_layout, create_runtime_descriptor_set, descriptor-aware create_runtime_pipeline_layout, record_runtime_descriptor_set_binding, create_runtime_compute_pipeline, record_runtime_compute_pipeline_binding, and record_runtime_compute_dispatch for move-only descriptor layout/pool/set ownership, compute pipeline ownership, command-buffer binding, and dispatch, record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_clear for shader-free swapchain clear recording, record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_draw and create_runtime_readback_buffer, record_runtime_swapchain_image_readback, and read_runtime_readback_buffer for move-only upload/readback ownership, upload-buffer CPU writes, swapchain image-to-buffer copy recording, and CPU byte mapping, build_swapchain_create_plan/build_swapchain_resize_plan for first-party swapchain/image-view/acquire/resize decisions, build_dynamic_rendering_plan for first-party extent, attachment format, and vkCmdBeginRendering/vkCmdEndRendering readiness checks, build_frame_synchronization_plan for first-party vkCmdPipelineBarrier2/vkQueueSubmit2 readiness plus acquire/render/readback/present ordering, build_texture_transition_barrier for standalone texture state hazards including transfer-write to shader-read visibility, depth_write attachment visibility, and depth_write-to-shader_read sampled-depth visibility, build_rhi_device_mapping_plan for first-party resource/swapchain/render-pass/pipeline/command-list/fence/readback/descriptor-set/compute-dispatch/visible-clear-readback/visible-draw-readback/visible-texture-sampling-readback/visible-depth-readback readiness before create_rhi_device exposes Vulkan as a complete visible IRhiDevice backend, and validate_spirv_shader_artifact for SPIR-V bytecode validation before native shader module creation without including Vulkan headers or exposing native handles. Native Vulkan sampled-image and sampler descriptor writes are implemented with private VkDescriptorImageInfo and VkSampler ownership through update_runtime_descriptor_set, and sampled-depth texture create/barrier planning and descriptor update coverage are implemented without exposing VkImageView or layouts, SDL3/Vulkan game-window presentation is validated on this Windows host through precompiled SPIR-V artifacts and real surface format/present-mode checks, environment-gated visible texture-sampling proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts, environment-gated depth-ordered draw/readback proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV and
