MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts, environment-gated postprocess-depth readback proof remains available when those depth-write artifacts plus MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts, environment-gated shadow receiver readback proof remains available when those depth-write artifacts plus MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts, environment-gated RhiFrameRenderer runtime material texture-sampling proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_FRAGMENT_SPV are configured, and environment-gated cooked runtime scene GPU palette proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real scene SPIR-V artifacts, and environment-gated compute dispatch readback proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_SPV points at a real compute SPIR-V artifact, and environment-gated Runtime RHI compute morph POSITION readback proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_SPV points at a real compute morph SPIR-V artifact, environment-gated Runtime RHI compute morph renderer-consumption proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_VERTEX_SPV and MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts, and environment-gated Runtime RHI compute morph NORMAL/TANGENT readback proof remains available when MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_TANGENT_FRAME_SPV points at a real tangent-frame compute SPIR-V artifact. create_runtime_device resolves optional vkSetDebugUtilsObjectNameEXT through vkGetDeviceProcAddr and applies best-effort GameEngine.RHI.Vulkan.* debug object names to the logical device, graphics and present queues, swapchains, images, image views, buffers, command pools, primary command buffers, shader modules, pipeline layouts, graphics pipelines, samplers, and related runtime handles when the ICD exports the entry point (null proc skips naming).
gameCodeGuidance.currentVulkanRuntimeOwners: Vulkan runtime owners now include VulkanRuntimeInstance, VulkanRuntimeDevice, VulkanRuntimeCommandPool, VulkanRuntimeBuffer, VulkanRuntimeTexture, VulkanRuntimeShaderModule, VulkanRuntimeDescriptorSetLayout, VulkanRuntimeDescriptorSet, VulkanRuntimePipelineLayout, VulkanRuntimeGraphicsPipeline, VulkanRuntimeComputePipeline, VulkanRuntimeSwapchain, VulkanRuntimeFrameSync, VulkanRuntimeReadbackBuffer, and VulkanRuntimeSampler. The create_rhi_device bridge exposes backend-neutral BufferHandle, TextureHandle, ShaderHandle, DescriptorSetLayoutHandle, DescriptorSetHandle, PipelineLayoutHandle, GraphicsPipelineHandle, ComputePipelineHandle, SwapchainHandle, SwapchainFrameHandle, graphics command lists, immediate FenceValue stats/waits, upload-buffer writes, descriptor updates for uniform/storage buffer, sampled texture, and sampler bindings, standalone texture transitions, buffer copies, buffer-to-texture copies, texture-to-buffer copies, standalone texture render-target clears, optional depth attachment clears, standalone texture render-target draws with optional depth attachments, copy-only submit, command-list-local texture state tracking, stale texture-state submit rejection, submit-success-only texture-state commits, and transient lease creation while keeping VkBuffer, VkImage, VkDeviceMemory, VkShaderModule, VkDescriptorSetLayout, VkDescriptorPool, VkDescriptorSet, VkWriteDescriptorSet, VkDescriptorBufferInfo, VkDescriptorImageInfo, VkSampler, VkBufferCopy, VkBufferImageCopy, VkPipelineLayout, VkPipeline, VkSurfaceKHR, VkSwapchainKHR, VkImageView, VkSemaphore, VkFence, and native commands private. Descriptor creation, update, and binding use create_runtime_descriptor_set_layout, create_runtime_descriptor_set, update_runtime_descriptor_set, descriptor-aware create_runtime_pipeline_layout, and record_runtime_descriptor_set_binding while VkDescriptorSetLayout, VkDescriptorPool, VkDescriptorSet, VkWriteDescriptorSet, VkDescriptorBufferInfo, vkUpdateDescriptorSets, vkCmdBindDescriptorSets, and Vulkan function pointers remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Pipeline creation uses create_runtime_pipeline_layout, create_runtime_graphics_pipeline, and create_runtime_compute_pipeline with dynamic rendering metadata, native DepthStencilStateDesc mapping, and compute shader modules while VkPipelineLayout, VkPipeline, VkPipelineVertexInputStateCreateInfo, VkGraphicsPipelineCreateInfo, VkComputePipelineCreateInfo, vertex input descriptions, and Vulkan function pointers remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Swapchain creation uses create_runtime_swapchain while VkSurfaceKHR, VkSwapchainKHR, VkImage, VkImageView, and image-view destruction order remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Frame sync, acquire, and present use create_runtime_frame_sync, acquire_next_runtime_swapchain_image, and present_runtime_swapchain_image while VkSemaphore, VkFence, VkPresentInfoKHR, vkAcquireNextImageKHR/vkQueuePresentKHR results, and acquired/presented image indices remain private or first-party mapped; the IRhiDevice bridge records shader-free swapchain clear render passes, queues present, submits through private synchronization2, and completes frame ownership on submit. Dynamic rendering color/depth clear and draw recording use record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_clear, record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_draw, record_runtime_texture_rendering_clear, and record_runtime_texture_rendering_draw while VkRenderingInfo, VkRenderingAttachmentInfo including pDepthAttachment, VkCommandBuffer, VkPipeline, VkImageView, viewport/scissor state, vertex/index buffers, and vkCmdBeginRendering/vkCmdBindPipeline/vkCmdBindVertexBuffers/vkCmdBindIndexBuffer/vkCmdDraw/vkCmdDrawIndexed/vkCmdEndRendering function pointers remain private. Standalone texture transition/copy recording uses build_texture_transition_barrier, record_runtime_texture_barrier, record_runtime_buffer_copy, record_runtime_buffer_texture_copy, and record_runtime_texture_buffer_copy while shader-read hazards, depth_write hazards, D24S8 depth|stencil image-barrier aspects, usage/format/state compatibility checks, VkImageMemoryBarrier2, VkDependencyInfo, VkBufferCopy, VkBufferImageCopy, vkCmdCopyBuffer, vkCmdCopyBufferToImage, vkCmdCopyImageToBuffer, and image layouts remain private or first-party mapped. Synchronization2 recording uses record_runtime_swapchain_frame_barrier and submit_runtime_command_buffer while VkDependencyInfo, VkImageMemoryBarrier2, VkSubmitInfo2, VkSemaphoreSubmitInfo, VkCommandBufferSubmitInfo, queue handles, semaphores, fences, and raw VkResult values remain private or first-party mapped; requested queue-idle waits must succeed before submit_runtime_command_buffer reports submitted, and logical device creation requires the synchronization2 feature before those commands are requested. Upload and readback use write_runtime_buffer, read_runtime_buffer, create_runtime_readback_buffer, record_runtime_swapchain_image_readback, and read_runtime_readback_buffer while VkBuffer, VkDeviceMemory, VkMemoryRequirements, VkPhysicalDeviceMemoryProperties, VkBufferImageCopy, mapped pointers, and native memory commands remain private or first-party mapped. Environment-gated visible draw/readback, raw texture-sampling, depth-ordered draw/readback, sampled-depth readback, shadow receiver readback, RhiFrameRenderer runtime material texture-sampling, and cooked runtime scene GPU palette proofs now run through IRhiDevice when MK_VULKAN_TEST_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_TANGENT_FRAME_SPV, or MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts and the local Vulkan runtime is available; default generated-game availability remains gated by runtime and shader-toolchain readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentMetal: SDK-independent MK_rhi_metal scaffold exposes backend identity, host support, capability profile, probe plan, deterministic probe result helpers, non-empty metallib shader library artifact validation, runtime/default-device/command-queue/shader-library readiness planning, texture synchronization planning for render/blit/present encoder intent, platform availability diagnostics, and Apple-only native default-device, command-queue, command-buffer, render-encoder, texture-target, CAMetalLayer drawable acquisition, drawable render-encoder creation, drawable present scheduling, command-buffer submission, and texture-readback ownership through retained private PIMPL objects without including Metal or QuartzCore headers or exposing native handles. Apple Objective-C++ paths assign GameEngine.RHI.Metal.* GPU debugger labels through -[MTLObject setLabel:] on the default MTLDevice, MTLCommandQueue, per-allocation MTLCommandBuffer, offscreen and drawable-backed MTLTexture targets, MTLRenderCommandEncoder, and transient readback MTLCommandBuffer/MTLBlitCommandEncoder/MTLBuffer objects.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInput: VirtualInput for keyboard-style state, VirtualPointerInput with PointerSample/PointerState for mouse, touch, and pen-style pointer state, VirtualGamepadInput with GamepadButton/GamepadAxis state for gamepad buttons and axes, mobile touch sample mapping into first-party PointerSample values without native handles, VirtualPointerInput::touch_gesture for active touch count, centroid, centroid delta, and two-touch pinch scale primitives, TouchGestureRecognizer for tap, double-tap, long-press, pan, swipe, pinch, rotate, and canceled gesture events, and MK_runtime RuntimeInputActionMap plus RuntimeInputStateView and RuntimeInputContextStack for deterministic GameEngine.RuntimeInputActions keyboard, pointer, gamepad button, scalar key-pair, and scalar gamepad-axis action documents evaluated over first-party virtual input state through bind_key, bind_pointer, bind_gamepad_button, bind_key_axis, bind_gamepad_axis, bind_key_in_context, bind_pointer_in_context, bind_gamepad_button_in_context, bind_key_axis_in_context, bind_gamepad_axis_in_context, context-aware action_down, and context-aware axis_value. Use RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action to turn the next allowed first-party key, pointer, or gamepad-button press into a validated RuntimeInputRebindingProfile action override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_axis to capture the first deterministic gamepad axis magnitude past a deadzone into a validated axis override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action_with_focus when a host-owned menu has armed one capture row and needs deterministic focus_retained plus gameplay_input_consumed output for waiting, captured, or pressed-but-rejected candidates. Use RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationModel rows from make_runtime_input_rebinding_presentation when a runtime/game menu needs reviewed base/profile action and axis display labels plus symbolic glyph lookup keys. Empty context stacks use default; non-empty stacks select the first context that declares the requested action. Scalar action axes apply deterministic clamp/deadzone handling; broad UI focus integration, global input consumption/bubbling beyond focused rebinding capture, radial stick deadzones, response curves, pointer delta axes, gesture-to-RuntimeInputActions binding, full runtime/game rebinding panels outside the reviewed editor action capture lane, real platform input glyph generation/assets/rendering, keyboard-layout localization, keyboard key-pair axis capture, multiplayer device assignment, per-device profiles, SDL3, and native device handles remain follow-up adapter work.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInputContextPlanning: Use RuntimeInputContextStackRequest, RuntimeInputContextLayerDesc, RuntimeInputContextStackPlan, and plan_runtime_input_context_stack before evaluating RuntimeInputActionMap when generated games combine gameplay, menu, dialogue, rebinding, capture, or passive overlay layers. sample_2d_playable_foundation is the source-tree adoption reference for a passive HUD overlay plus gameplay stack. The planner validates context names, rejects duplicate contexts, supports default-context fallback, blocks lower-priority layers for modal rows, and reports gameplay_input_available, gameplay_input_consumed, ui_context_active, and capture_context_active without native handles, platform adapters, game-specific menu schemas, or a full UI focus/navigation system.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInputRebindingProfiles: MK_runtime RuntimeInputRebindingProfile and GameEngine.RuntimeInputRebindingProfile provide deterministic persisted full-row action and axis overlays over a caller-supplied base RuntimeInputActionMap. Use validate_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, apply_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, serialize_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, deserialize_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, load_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, and write_runtime_input_rebinding_profile through IFileSystem/RootedFileSystem. Use RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action for host-independent digital action trigger capture from first-party keyboard, pointer, and gamepad-button state into a validated action override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_axis for host-independent gamepad-axis and two-held-keyboard key-pair axis capture (capture_keyboard_key_pair_axes) from first-party VirtualGamepadInput axis magnitudes and VirtualInput key-down pairs into a validated axis override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action_with_focus for an armed/focused/modal capture guard that reports focus_retained while waiting and gameplay_input_consumed for waiting, captured, or pressed-but-rejected digital candidates after the guard passes. Use RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationToken, RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationRow, RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationModel, present_runtime_input_action_trigger, present_runtime_input_axis_source, and make_runtime_input_rebinding_presentation for host-independent display rows with deterministic labels and symbolic glyph lookup keys after loading, validating, or applying a profile. Visible full interactive runtime/game rebinding panels outside the reviewed editor action capture lane, broad UI focus integration, global input consumption/bubbling beyond focused rebinding capture, radial stick deadzones, response curves, pointer delta axes, gesture-to-RuntimeInputActions binding, real platform input glyph generation/assets/rendering, keyboard-layout localization, keyboard key-pair axis capture, multiplayer device assignment, per-device profiles, SDL3 input APIs, editor-private runtime APIs, native device handles, and cloud/binary saves remain follow-up adapter work.
gameCodeGuidance.currentProcessExecution: ProcessCommand, RecordingProcessRunner, run_process_command, RootedFileSystem, and Windows Win32ProcessRunner for shell-free executable-plus-arguments process execution with stdout/stderr capture and rooted project file access
gameCodeGuidance.currentToolExecution: ShaderToolProcessRunner and ShaderToolExecutionPolicy bridge shader compile commands to platform process execution with artifact output-root policy and reviewed executable override for PATH names, project-relative tool paths, or absolute installed SDK/tool paths; ShaderArtifactValidationProcessRunner bridges spirv-val validation commands to the same shell-free process policy; DXC Vulkan SPIR-V command planning uses -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3 through executable-plus-argument vectors; MK_editor_core make_viewport_shader_compile_requests queues default D3D12 DXIL and Vulkan SPIR-V viewport shader artifacts from one HLSL source, while make_material_preview_shader_compile_requests queues factor-only and base-color-textured material preview D3D12 DXIL/Vulkan SPIR-V artifacts from a material-preview HLSL source; discover_shader_tools finds deterministic dxc, spirv-val, metal, and metallib descriptors and tracks dxc.spirv-codegen capability markers for deterministic toolchain roots; evaluate_shader_toolchain_readiness reports D3D12 DXIL, Vulkan SPIR-V, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen, and Metal library readiness; editor ProjectShaderToolSettings executable values may be reviewed PATH tool names, project-relative tool paths, or absolute installed SDK/tool paths while working directory, artifact root, and cache index paths stay project-relative; make_spirv_shader_validation_command and execute_shader_artifact_validation_action provide reviewed SPIR-V artifact validation before native Vulkan shader module promotion; MK_editor_core ShaderToolDiscoveryState exposes discovered tools and readiness diagnostics to editor UI/AI workflows; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1 reports local shader tool blockers, reads Process/User/Machine environment state, searches MK_SHADER_TOOLCHAIN_ROOT, User/Machine PATH, Vulkan SDK variables, and vcpkg tool roots before Windows SDK fallback, runs a real quoted DXC SPIR-V CodeGen probe, and tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1 -RequireAll can gate CI lanes; MK_editor_core ViewportShaderArtifactState verifies non-empty compiled D3D12 VS/PS DXIL bytecode and Vulkan VS/PS SPIR-V artifacts before native viewport promotion and can query readiness by shader id for material preview variants; build_shader_artifact_provenance records compiler, command, artifact, and input fingerprints for cache invalidation; ShaderArtifactCacheIndex and execute_shader_compile_action provide persistent shader cache index storage, cache-hit reuse, .geprovenance writes, deterministic test artifact marker writes, and real artifact enforcement for compiler-backed runs; build_shader_hot_reload_plan and build_shader_pipeline_recreation_plan turn stale provenance or successful non-cached compiles into explicit pipeline recreation decisions; build_shader_pipeline_cache_plan batches selected compile requests into deterministic hot-reload, compile-required, pipeline-recreation, cache-entry-current, and cache-index-repair rows; reconcile_shader_pipeline_cache_index repairs selected first-party cache indexes from current provenance without invoking compilers or writing artifacts; execute_asset_import_plan runs asset import or recook plans through IFileSystem, decodes first-party texture/mesh/audio/material source documents including optional hex byte payloads, optionally routes PNG, glTF, and common audio sources through MK_tools-owned asset-importers adapters when MK_ENABLE_ASSET_IMPORTERS is enabled, writes deterministic cooked artifacts preserving first-party byte payloads, decoded PNG RGBA8 payloads, decoded common-audio PCM payloads, and glTF triangle POSITION-only or POSITION/NORMAL/TEXCOORD_0/TANGENT interleaved mesh byte payloads for embedded/loaded buffers while rejecting partial normal/UV/tangent-frame primitives; execute_asset_runtime_recook builds recook plans, executes imports transactionally, stages successful cooked artifacts through AssetRuntimeReplacementState, and rolls every requested asset back on recook transaction failure; execute_asset_runtime_package_hot_reload_replacement_safe_point runs reviewed recook execution, passes helper-owned staged rows into commit_runtime_package_hot_reload_recook_replacement, and commits only selected package matched AssetRuntimeReplacementState rows after resident replacement success; scan_asset_files_for_hot_reload turns registered asset files into deterministic hot reload snapshots; PollingFileWatcher turns IFileSystem roots into deterministic added, modified, and removed file events for AssetHotReloadRecookScheduler inputs; WindowsFileWatcher turns Win32 API ReadDirectoryChangesW notifications into FileWatchEvent records; LinuxFileWatcher turns inotify notifications into FileWatchEvent records; MacOSFileWatcher turns FSEvents notifications into FileWatchEvent records; native watcher handles stay backend-private and host-unavailable backends report explicit diagnostics
gameCodeGuidance.currentAssets: AssetRegistry, AssetDependencyGraph, AssetHotReloadTracker, AssetHotReloadRecookScheduler for debounce-based source and dependency-invalidated recook requests, AssetHotReloadApplyState for failed-reload rollback state, AssetRuntimeReplacementState for staging runtime asset replacements until a safe point, committing only selected staged assets, and rolling back failed recooks without losing the active asset, AssetCookedPackageIndex with content hashes, dependency edges, serialization, and deterministic recook decisions, deterministic GameEngine.Tilemap metadata through TilemapMetadataDocument with AssetKind::tilemap and tilemap_texture package edges while native GPU sprite batching, package-authored atlas metadata workflows beyond the reviewed decode/pack path, and production atlas editor UX remain follow-up, ShaderMetadataRegistry with descriptor reflection rows, shader compile command planning including DXC Vulkan SPIR-V commands with -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3, shader tool runner validation, compiler output capture contracts, shader include dependency discovery, shader include dependency graph edge generation, shader artifact manifest serialization including reflection rows, AssetImportMetadataRegistry, first-party texture/mesh/audio source document serialization with optional hex byte payloads, build_asset_import_plan, build_asset_recook_plan, MaterialDefinition, MaterialInstanceDefinition, deterministic material/material-instance serialization, material instance composition, fixed material factor uniform sizing, and explicit MaterialPipelineBindingMetadata for backend-neutral descriptor/pipeline layout derivation with uniform-buffer, sampled-texture, and sampler rows; optional PNG/glTF/common-audio source import adapters are implemented in MK_tools behind the asset-importers feature and feed this first-party cooked-artifact path; MK_tools preserves first-party texture data_hex, mesh vertex/index data_hex, and audio data_hex into deterministic cooked artifacts, decodes PNG RGBA8 payloads into texture data_hex, decodes common-audio PCM samples into audio data_hex, and cooks glTF triangle POSITION-only or POSITION/NORMAL/TEXCOORD_0/TANGENT interleaved accessors plus indexed/generated indices into deterministic mesh.vertex_data_hex and uint32 mesh.index_data_hex for embedded/loaded buffers; MK_runtime now reads cooked package indexes through IFileSystem, validates runtime payload hashes and dependencies, exposes first-party runtime asset handles, provides typed cooked texture/mesh/audio/material/scene/tilemap payload access including decoded optional byte payloads, serialized scene text, tilemap atlas texture path checks, and tilemap_texture edge inspection, resolves scene material dependencies through resolve_runtime_scene_materials, stages all-or-nothing package replacement at safe points, owns host-independent save/settings/localization/input action session documents, and exposes RuntimeDiagnosticReport helpers for package load, payload, scene, and session failures; MK_runtime_rhi creates backend-neutral RHI texture resources, mesh vertex/index buffer upload paths with derived position-only stride 12 or lit tangent-space stride 48 metadata, 256-byte-backed material factor uniform buffers with 64-byte factor payloads and one-pass Frame Graph command-list evidence, sampler-aware owner-device-validated material descriptor bindings, and submitted_fence rows from typed runtime payload uploads; MK_runtime_scene_rhi turns cooked package scene packets into retained SceneGpuBindingPalette resources through those typed runtime payload parsers and RHI upload helpers, preserves actual submit-order submitted_upload_fences rows, and reports submitted_upload_fence_count plus last_submitted_upload_fence rows; MK_editor_core can turn selected cooked material/texture artifacts into a non-throwing GPU binding preview plan using typed runtime payload parsers; and renderer MeshCommand bindings can now consume those RHI handles for descriptor binding, vertex/index binding, and indexed draws through SceneGpuBindingPalette

two-bone 3D orientation basis output via AnimationTwoBoneIk3dDesc, AnimationTwoBoneIk3dJointFrame, AnimationTwoBoneIk3dSolution, and solve_animation_two_bone_ik_3d_orientation, and deterministic multi-segment XY FABRIK chain IK via AnimationFabrikIkXyBendSide, AnimationFabrikIkXyDesc::bend_side, AnimationFabrikIkXyDesc::pole_vector_xy, AnimationFabrikIkXySegmentRotationLimit, AnimationFabrikIkXySolution, solve_animation_fabrik_ik_xy_chain, AnimationIkLocalRotationLimit, and apply_animation_fabrik_ik_xy_solution_to_pose for optional explicit bend-side plus finite non-collinear pole-vector branch selection, solver-integrated segment local Z limits, signed-pi local angle normalization, and local pose write-back clamps, plus deterministic multi-segment 3D FABRIK chain IK via AnimationFabrikIk3dDesc, AnimationFabrikIk3dSolution, and solve_animation_fabrik_ik_3d_chain for root-fixed 3D joint positions, and apply_animation_fabrik_ik_3d_solution_to_pose for validated AnimationPose3d unit-quaternion local rotation write-back, plus Animation 3D Local Rotation Limits Twist Controls v1 through AnimationIkLocalRotationLimit3d, validate_animation_ik_local_rotation_limits_3d, apply_animation_local_rotation_limits_3d, and the local-limit overload of apply_animation_fabrik_ik_3d_solution_to_pose for deterministic swing/twist clamps and constraint reporting in AnimationPose3d, plus Animation Root Motion Foundation v1 through AnimationRootMotionSampleDesc, AnimationRootMotionSample, AnimationRootMotionAccumulationDesc, AnimationRootMotionAccumulation, AnimationRootMotionDiagnostic, validate_animation_root_motion_sample, is_valid_animation_root_motion_sample, sample_animation_root_motion, validate_animation_root_motion_accumulation, is_valid_animation_root_motion_accumulation, and accumulate_animation_root_motion for root-joint translation and Z-rotation start/end/delta sampling plus explicit-duration forward loop accumulation. MK_assets provides authored GameEngine.AnimationTransformBindingSource binding source rows through AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument for mapping scalar curve targets to named transform components. MK_tools can derive those rows from glTF node TRS targets through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_binding_source using glTF node names or deterministic fallback names, and can import full unit-quaternion 3D node transform tracks through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_tracks_3d and bridge them into cooked quaternion clip source rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_quaternion_clip. MK_assets exposes GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource and GameEngine.CookedAnimationQuaternionClip for selected 3D TRS tracks, and MK_runtime exposes runtime_animation_quaternion_clip_payload for cooked package records. MK_runtime_scene resolves scalar binding rows for RuntimeSceneInstance data through resolve_runtime_scene_animation_transform_bindings, can apply caller-provided FloatAnimationCurveSample rows through apply_runtime_scene_animation_transform_samples, and can apply validated AnimationPose3d rows to named RuntimeSceneInstance nodes through apply_runtime_scene_animation_pose_3d. MK_scene_renderer can sample a cooked scalar AnimationFloatClipSourceDocument and apply AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance data through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_float_clip, then rebuild the render packet for generated 3D package smoke validation. MK_scene_renderer can also sample AnimationJointTrack3dDesc rows, apply named-joint AnimationPose3d rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance scene nodes, and rebuild the render packet through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_pose_3d. MK_scene_renderer can also sample cooked scalar morph-weight clips against RuntimeMorphMeshCpuPayload rows through sample_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu_animation_float_clip and return deterministic CPU-morphed positions for generated 3D package smoke validation. Generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and sample_desktop_runtime_game can load cooked animation_quaternion_clip records through runtime_animation_quaternion_clip_payload, decode them with make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes, and validate --require-quaternion-animation through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_pose_3d plus quaternion_animation_scene_applied/quaternion_animation_scene_rotation_z counters. JointLocalTransform remains the Z-only local pose contract for existing clips, while AnimationJointLocalTransform3d covers translation, positive scale, and unit-quaternion local rotations for 3D model-pose construction; AnimationJointTrack3dDesc can sample selected translation, quaternion rotation, and positive-scale tracks into AnimationPose3d with rest-pose fallback for missing components, and AnimationJointTrack3dByteSource plus make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes decode cooked little-endian component byte rows into those descriptors. MK_tools can import LINEAR glTF node TRS 3D rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_tracks_3d with full unit-quaternion rotation keyframes for caller-mapped AnimationPose3d workflows and can emit GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_quaternion_clip. Backward root-motion accumulation, negative-time wrapping, inferred clip duration, tangent-space material/render integration beyond the cooked tangent-frame schema, broader GPU skinning parity, broader IK constraints beyond the implemented planar segment local Z and 3D swing/twist local clamps, authored IK assets, and animation graph authoring are not implemented yet.
gameCodeGuidance.currentAudio: AudioMixer with logical buses, voices, asset-id clip references, clip metadata registration, deterministic mix plans, spatial_mix_plan for listener/source distance attenuation and stereo pan metadata, AudioStreamingQueue for decoded chunk append/consume/starvation state, sample-frame render scheduling, streaming buffer availability, device format conversion intent, underrun diagnostics, AudioClipSampleData validation, selectable nearest/linear resampling quality, and render_interleaved_float output with gain, channel conversion, and clamped interleaved float PCM. AudioGameplayMixRequest, AudioGameplayMixPlan, and plan_gameplay_audio_mix provide the fail-closed game-facing cue/bus/trigger planner: game-owned music, sfx, ambience, ui, voice, and custom cue rows can describe clip ids, buses, gain, looping, pause/mute/fade, trigger start frames, gain scales, and optional spatial metadata before converting into mixer-ready AudioBusDesc and AudioVoiceDesc commands without native handles or codec assumptions. AudioDeviceStreamRequest, AudioDeviceStreamPlan, plan_audio_device_stream, and render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float provide the device-independent stream pump baseline: host adapters report current queued frames and a target, MK_audio schedules bounded render chunks at device_frame + queued_frames, returns no_work when the queue is already full enough, and rejects invalid formats, invalid queue/render budgets, invalid resampling quality, and device-frame overflow. Selected 2D/3D package samples can require --require-audio-gameplay-mixer and load cooked package audio payloads before reporting audio_gameplay_mixer_ready, bus/cue/trigger/command rows, paused/faded buses, looping/spatial commands, render frame/sample counters, sample absolute sums, and sample/payload diagnostics from first-party planner/mixer evidence without native device or codec breadth claims. Optional MK_audio_sdl3 SdlAudioRuntime/SdlAudioDevice opens SDL3 default playback streams through SDL_OpenAudioDeviceStream, queues silence or rendered interleaved float PCM, reports queued frames, and exposes pause/resume/clear controls without public SDL handles; platform/android contains a private AAudio output adapter that opens a low-latency float32 output stream, uses an atomic FIFO for callback-safe sample transfer, and starts/stops with Android lifecycle events without public AAudio handles; codecs, streaming decode integration, HRTF, broader DSP graph execution, device hotplug/selection, mixer authoring UI, and richer codec workflows remain follow-up work

gameCodeGuidance.currentNavigation: NavigationGrid, NavigationGridCoord, NavigationPathRequest, NavigationPathResult, NavigationPathStatus, and find_navigation_path provide a standard-library-only deterministic cardinal-grid A* foundation with walkability, positive traversal costs, stable tie-breaking, invalid/blocked endpoint diagnostics, no-path reporting, total path cost, and visited-node counts. NavigationPoint2, NavigationPointPathBuildResult, build_navigation_point_path_result, build_navigation_point_path, NavigationPathFollowRequest, advance_navigation_path_following, NavigationArriveSteeringRequest, and calculate_navigation_arrive_steering provide Path Following / Steering Foundation v0 for deterministic grid-path-to-point conversion, explicit target cursor advancement, max-step and arrival-radius movement, advanced/remaining distance reporting, and single-agent arrive steering desired velocity/speed. NavigationAgentConfig, NavigationAgentState, NavigationAgentUpdateRequest, NavigationAgentUpdateResult, make_navigation_agent_state, replace_navigation_agent_path, cancel_navigation_agent_move, and update_navigation_agent provide Navigation Agent Controller Foundation v0 for deterministic value-type agent movement state/ticks with idle/moving/reached/cancelled/invalid statuses, speed/delta clamping, final-destination slowing, path replacement, remaining-distance reporting, and desired velocity from actual path movement. NavigationGridPathValidationRequest, NavigationGridPathValidationResult, NavigationGridPathValidationStatus, validate_navigation_grid_path, NavigationGridReplanRequest, NavigationGridReplanResult, NavigationGridReplanStatus, and replan_navigation_grid_path provide Grid Dynamic Obstacle Replan v0 for validating remaining cardinal-grid paths against the current grid, reporting empty/start/goal/out-of-bounds/blocked/non-cardinal/unsupported-adjacency/cost-overflow diagnostics, reusing valid remaining paths, and recalculating deterministic replacement paths around newly blocked cells through find_navigation_path. NavigationLocalAvoidanceRequest, NavigationLocalAvoidanceResult, NavigationLocalAvoidanceDiagnostic, and calculate_navigation_local_avoidance provide Navigation Agent Local Avoidance v0 for finite-input single-agent desired velocity adjustment against explicit nearby neighbor rows, duplicate-neighbor and calculation-overflow diagnostics, deterministic neighbor ordering, closest-approach prediction over the requested time window, overlap/future-collision separation, applied-neighbor counts, and max-speed clamping. NavigationGridPathSmoothingRequest, NavigationGridPathSmoothingResult, NavigationGridPathSmoothingStatus, and smooth_navigation_grid_path provide Navigation Grid Path Smoothing v0 for reducing validated cardinal grid paths through deterministic walkable line-of-sight while preserving start and goal waypoints. NavigationGridAgentPathRequest, NavigationGridAgentPathPlan, NavigationGridAgentPathStatus, NavigationGridAgentPathDiagnostic, and plan_navigation_grid_agent_path provide Navigation Production Path v1 by composing pathfinding, optional smoothing, point-path mapping, diagnostics, point-count/cost evidence, and NavigationAgentState creation for generated-game route setup. NavigationNavmeshPathRequest, NavigationNavmeshPathResult, NavigationNavmeshDynamicObstacle, NavigationNavmeshReadinessConfig, NavigationNavmeshReadinessReport, plan_navigation_navmesh_path, and evaluate_navigation_navmesh_readiness provide Navigation Navmesh Production Path v1 by composing caller-authored scene-ref polygon rows, portal rows, deterministic dynamic-obstacle blocking, point-path mapping, status diagnostics, visited counts, cost evidence, and value-only readiness diagnostics for generated-game smoke proof. NavigationCrowdAgentDesc, NavigationCrowdPlanRequest, NavigationCrowdAgentPlanRow, NavigationCrowdPlanResult, NavigationCrowdPlanStatus, NavigationCrowdPlanDiagnostic, NavigationCrowdReadinessConfig, NavigationCrowdReadinessReport, NavigationCrowdReadinessStatus, NavigationCrowdReadinessDiagnostic, plan_navigation_navmesh_crowd, and evaluate_navigation_crowd_readiness provide Navigation Navmesh Crowd Readiness v1 for value-only deterministic crowd rows over navmesh routes, dynamic-obstacle participation, local-avoidance aggregation, max-agent budget rejection, duplicate/invalid-agent diagnostics, source-order checks, row budgets, readiness summaries, and stable replay counters. Generated games can use these for tile/grid movement, selected scene-ref polygon routing, value-only crowd movement authoring, and headless AI tests. navmesh asset import, spline/funnel smoothing, persistent/full crowd simulation beyond value-only batch planning, animation-aware steering, networked crowd determinism, RVO/ORCA, scene/physics integration, editor visualization, middleware, and background navigation jobs are not implemented yet.

Continue audio from the implemented device-independent gameplay cue/bus planner, spatial planning, decoded streaming queue, scheduling/conversion/underrun/selectable-resampling/float-PCM render baseline plus optional SDL3 desktop and Android AAudio output adapters into streaming decode integration, HRTF/spatial quality, broader DSP graphs, device hotplug/selection, mixer authoring UI, and richer format conversion quality gates.

Continue physics from the implemented 2D/3D broadphase, 2D circle contacts, 3D sphere/capsule contacts, exact 3D AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule shape sweeps, exact sphere convenience casts, deterministic contact manifolds through PhysicsWorld3D::contact_manifolds, opt-in deterministic CCD rows through PhysicsWorld3D::step_continuous, layers/masks, conservative capsule character movement, in-memory and package-authored collision rows, iterative solver configuration, stacked-body coverage, replay-tested contact resolution, and documented Jolt-preferred optional backend decision, plus generated-game character/dynamic policy rows, explicit distance-joint solving, and count-based determinism gates, into the optional `physics-jolt` adapter lane or explicit default/generated-game 1.0 exclusion before adding broader middleware; continue animation from implemented blend tree samples, 1D blend graph samples, layered samples, curve binding samples, authored event tracks, retargeting policy, skeleton/local-pose contracts, skin payload/model-pose/palette contracts, CPU skinned-position deformation, optional CPU skinned-normal streams, optional CPU skinned-tangent streams, optional CPU skinned-bitangent reconstruction, CPU morph target streams, first-party morph_mesh_cpu package rows, glTF morph-weight clip import bridge, generated 3D morph package consumption smoke, planar two-bone IK with explicit bend-side plus pole-vector branch selection, XY FABRIK chain IK, XY FABRIK pose application, local IK rotation limits, solver-integrated XY FABRIK segment local Z limits, signed-pi IK local angle normalization, multi-segment XY FABRIK bend-side/pole-vector branch selection, analytic two-bone 3D orientation basis output, deterministic 3D FABRIK joint-position solving, 3D FABRIK solution -> unit-quaternion local pose write-back, unit-quaternion 3D local-pose/model-pose matrices, quaternion keyframe sampling and selected 3D joint-track local-pose application, LINEAR glTF node TRS 3D quaternion import, cooked quaternion clip source/cook/runtime payloads, runtime-scene quaternion pose transform application, 3D local rotation limits/twist controls, root-motion sampling/forward accumulation, package-visible compute morph queue-wait smoke evidence, runtime-scene RHI skin+compute-morph palette composition, generated package skin+compute smoke, RHI compute-morph async telemetry, generated package async telemetry, tangent-space material/render integration, cooked animation asset workflows, and authored animation asset workflows.

Continue live-iteration work from the implemented file watcher backend selection, polling fallback, Windows ReadDirectoryChangesW backend, Linux inotify backend, macOS FSEvents backend, debounced recook scheduling, dependency invalidation, failed-reload rollback state, safe runtime replacement staging, editor/core plus GUI diagnostic state, editor/runtime recook execution, shader hot reload planning, and renderer pipeline replacement into broader cross-platform CI coverage and runtime/editor workflow hardening.

Continue first-party retained-mode MK_ui runtime UI, MK_ui_renderer, and MK_editor_ui editor model contracts from the implemented headless document/focus/style/text/image/layout/transition/binding/renderer-submission/text-image-accessibility-payload/monospace-text-layout/IRenderer-adapter plus UiRendererGlyphAtlasPalette glyph sprite baseline and UiRendererImagePalette colored sprite placeholder baseline, Dear ImGui asset-pipeline adapter, timeline adapter, and recorder-backed profiler adapter into concrete renderer text/image/font/accessibility adapters, richer layout models, localization/accessibility metadata, and additional editor-shell adapters while keeping generated games on public mirakana::ui APIs instead of Dear ImGui, SDL3, UI middleware, or editor-private APIs.

Continue mobile from the implemented SDK-independent lifecycle/touch/orientation/safe-area/storage/permission contracts, Android GameActivity/NDK/Prefab template, Android Release Device Matrix v1 sample_headless Debug/Release/signing/API36-emulator evidence, Android native Vulkan surface creation bridge, Android AAudio output adapter, Android resource packaging/signing verification, deterministic Android AVD diagnostics, Android emulator smoke, iOS CMake/Xcode bundle template, iOS Metal-backed root view, iOS storage roots, iOS resource packaging/signing inputs, iOS Simulator smoke script, dedicated iOS GitHub Actions lane, blocker-aware pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-mobile-packaging.ps1 diagnostics, and Apple Metal iOS Host Evidence v1 apple-host-evidence-check host-gated diagnostics into actual Apple-hosted iOS simulator/device validation results, backend-neutral Apple Metal presentation, iOS signing validation, production Android signing/upload policy, physical-device coverage, broader ABI/device matrix coverage, and broader device/simulator CI lanes.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dSceneCollisionPackageSmo: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-scene-collision-package on a selected installed package smoke that loads GameEngine.PhysicsCollisionScene3D as AssetKind::physics_collision_scene, preserves backend.native=unsupported, decodes RuntimePhysicsCollisionScene3DPayload rows with material and body.N.compound metadata, builds PhysicsWorld3D through build_physics_world_3d_from_runtime_collision_scene, and requires collision_package_status=ready, collision_package_ready=1, collision_package_diagnostics=0, collision_package_bodies=3, collision_package_triggers=1, positive contact/trigger-overlap evidence, collision_package_world_ready=1, collision_query_batch_ready=1, collision_query_batch_source_order_ready=1, collision_query_readiness_status=ready, collision_query_readiness_diagnostic=none, collision_query_readiness_diagnostics=0, deterministic collision query batch hit/no-hit/invalid-request counters, query-budget rejection counters, and gameplay_systems_collision_package_ready=1. This is a narrow first-party collision package smoke; exact AABB/sphere/vertical-capsule runtime queries now live in PhysicsWorld3D::exact_shape_sweep, deterministic collision query batches now live in PhysicsWorld3D::raycast_batch and PhysicsWorld3D::shape_sweep_batch, selected query readiness summaries now live in evaluate_physics_collision_query_readiness_3d, deterministic contact manifold rows now live in PhysicsWorld3D::contact_manifolds, opt-in CCD rows now live in PhysicsWorld3D::step_continuous, generated-game character/dynamic policy rows now live in evaluate_physics_character_dynamic_policy_3d, explicit distance-joint solving now lives in solve_physics_joints_3d, and opt-in distance/fixed/linear-axis positional constraint solving now lives in solve_physics_constraints_3d with default-unbounded max_rows row budgets and row_budget_exceeded diagnostics, value-only kinematic motion planning now lives in plan_physics_kinematic_motion_3d with deterministic slide/trigger/ground-probe rows, simple vehicle policy planning now lives in plan_physics_simple_vehicle_3d with deterministic wheel-probe rows, while host-independent replay signatures and count-based budget diagnostics now live in make_physics_replay_signature_3d and evaluate_physics_determinism_gate_3d, but dynamic-vs-dynamic TOI, rotational CCD, rotational rigid-body constraints, 2D CCD, persistent joint assets, broad vehicle dynamics/persistent vehicle simulation APIs, ragdolls, Jolt/native backends in the default/generated-game ready surface, broader middleware parity, and broad physics beyond the first-party Physics 1.0 ready surface remain separate follow-up work while `MK_physics_jolt` stays optional and dependency-gated.
gameCodeGuidance.currentRuntimeSimulationPersistence: Generated games can use mirakana::runtime::RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRequest, RuntimeSimulationPersistencePlan, RuntimeSimulationPersistentEntityRow, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceMigrationStep, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceDiagnostic, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRemediationAction, and plan_runtime_simulation_persistence from MK_runtime after load_runtime_session_profile_documents when selected simulation resume needs stable save-slot, world id, snapshot id, world tick, entity state rows, supported schema migration chains, or corrupt-save remediation evidence. Non-save profile document failures do not block valid save recovery. The planner is value-only, reports RuntimeSimulationPersistenceDiagnosticCode rows for blocking save document status, corrupt save documents, missing save.slot/world.id/snapshot.id/world.tick evidence, malformed entity.<id>.(type|region|state_hash) rows, missing required entities, unsupported schema versions, invalid migration rows, and missing migration-chain steps, and leaves filesystem/package state untouched. RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRemediationAction is a recommendation only. This is not a cloud save system, cross-device sync, multiplayer replication layer, binary compatibility policy, platform user-directory resolver, migration executor, filesystem/package mutation helper, native handle surface, or game-specific component serializer beyond reviewed save keys.

steps: For AI Game Design Spec v1 generated 2D/3D package work, write or update fail-closed game.agent.json aiWorkflow.gameDesignSpec rows before implementation so gameplay family, template, camera, input map, core loop, scene list, asset requests, systems, package targets, validation recipe ids, quality gates, and unsupported claims reference only same-manifest package targets and validation recipes.

For AI Safe Content Mutation Ledger v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.contentMutationLedger rows fail-closed with reviewed mutation ownership, forbidden shared paths, and explicit package/CMake registration surfaces before writing generated game content.

For AI Placeholder Asset Pipeline v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.placeholderAssetPipeline rows limited to first-party placeholder assets, deterministic replacementWorkflow rows, package handoff counters, and no external asset downloads.

For AI Generated Game Playtest Loop v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.generatedGamePlaytestLoop rows tied to reviewed validation recipe surfaces, failure classification, and no validation weakening.

For AI Validation Remediation Recipes v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.validationRemediationRecipes rows scoped to common missing package file, invalid reference, host gate, shader/tool gap, counter mismatch, and runtime package load failures.

For ai-generated-game-quality-rubric-v1 work, keep game.agent.json aiWorkflow.generatedGameQualityRubric rows objective and evidence-based around objective, controls, feedback, deterministic-package-smoke, budget-evidence, unsupportedRows, and broad production readiness exclusions.

CI Matrix Contract Check v1 keeps ciMatrixCheck and classifyPrValidationTier aligned with tools/check-ci-matrix.ps1, tools/classify-pr-validation-tier.ps1, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-ci-matrix.ps1, C++23 Release Package Artifact CI Evidence v1, Full Repository Static Analysis CI Contract v1, Assert-ReleasePackageArtifacts, Windows/Linux/sanitizer/macOS/iOS lane selection, static-analysis, and broader build/package CI execution evidence.
Use only public mirakana:: APIs from engine headers.

Use MK_core diagnostics/profile/trace export/review/reconstruction APIs for deterministic CPU observability; host adapters can attach a DiagnosticsRecorder and optional IProfileClock through DesktopHostServices to capture runtime_host.frame and renderer counters; use build_diagnostics_ops_plan plus export_diagnostics_trace_json, review_diagnostics_trace_json, and import_diagnostics_trace_json for dependency-free summary/trace/crash-review/telemetry handoff and first-party exported Trace Event JSON review/reconstruction instead of inventing game-local profiling formats, and do not expose native/backend handles.
Do not use Dear ImGui, SDL3, editor, UI middleware, or editor-private APIs for game runtime HUDs or menus; target first-party mirakana::ui contracts.

When a generated or sample game needs a reusable unsupported engine capability, stop at AI Engine Capability Handoff v1: use mirakana::EngineCapabilityHandoffRequestRow plus mirakana::review_engine_capability_handoff_request and optional game.agent.json aiWorkflow.engineCapabilityHandoffs descriptor rows to record the canonical capability id, blocked feature, current workaround, affected game files, desired first-party public contract, and required evidence without editing engine internals, exposing native handles, or leaking SDL3/Dear ImGui/renderer/RHI/backend/middleware contracts.

When authored source assets and Scene data need a host-independent runtime scene package, use the validated authored-to-runtime workflow register-source-asset -> cook-registered-source-assets -> migrate-scene-runtime-package -> validate-runtime-scene-package. Select validate-runtime-scene-package inputs from game.agent.json runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows after the package exists, and stop there unless a selected renderer or desktop recipe adds its own host-gated validation.

Register headless/default games with MK_add_game in games/CMakeLists.txt; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimePackage for config-only desktop package scaffolds, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimeCookedScenePackage for first-party cooked-scene desktop package scaffolds, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntime2DPackage for generated host-gated packaged 2D gameplay with cooked sprite/material/audio/orthographic scene payloads, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntime3DPackage for generated host-gated packaged 3D gameplay with cooked texture/mesh/morph_mesh_cpu/material/animation_float_clip/animation_quaternion_clip/perspective scene/physics-collision payloads plus transform/morph/quaternion scene-transform package smoke, selected safe-point package streaming smoke, selected renderer quality package smoke, selected scene collision package smoke, and primary camera/controller package smoke, pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/new-game.ps1 -Name <game_name> -Template DesktopRuntimeMaterialShaderPackage only when a scaffold needs source material/HLSL authoring inputs plus host-built desktop shader artifacts, or MK_add_desktop_runtime_game with GAME_MANIFEST plus mirakana::SdlDesktopGameHost only when selecting the optional desktop runtime lane, and declare config/assets/cooked package payloads in runtimePackageFiles with PACKAGE_FILES_FROM_MANIFEST so they ship beside the executable. Cooked-scene, packaged-2D, and packaged-3D manifests must also declare runtimeSceneValidationTargets rows with safe game-relative .geindex paths and explicit scene asset keys for validate-runtime-scene-package, plus host-gated-safe-point packageStreamingResidencyTargets rows that reference those validation targets and .geindex package files for selected safe-point package streaming execution. Source-tree desktop runtime builds stage registered package files and shader artifacts beside a target-specific executable directory to avoid cross-target generated payload collisions, and generated 2D/material-shader/3D package targets get DXC-available source-tree shader-artifact CTests before installed package validation when they declare selected D3D12 shader artifacts. Source material and HLSL files remain authoring inputs and must not be listed in runtimePackageFiles, runtimeSceneValidationTargets, or packageStreamingResidencyTargets. Use the editor Apply Package Registration button or tools/register-runtime-package-files.ps1 -GameManifest games/<game_name>/game.agent.json -RuntimePackageFile <game-relative-file> after reviewing package draft rows, then run schema/package validation. Use literal CMake PACKAGE_FILES only when intentionally mirroring the manifest.

Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/prepare-worktree.ps1 after entering a manual linked worktree so ignored worktree roots, external/vcpkg, and any existing local vcpkg_installed package tree are ready before configure. Register auto-merge with gh pr merge --auto --merge --match-head-commit <headRefOid> and leave branch deletion to cleanup because gh pr merge --delete-branch also deletes the local branch. After a task-owned PR is merged into main, run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/remove-merged-worktree.ps1 -WorktreePath <path> -BaseRef origin/main -BaseBranch main -Remote origin -DeleteLocalBranch -DeleteRemoteBranch from Local or another non-target checkout for guarded post-merge cleanup; it fetches/prunes origin, requires the local checkout to be clean and on the base branch, fast-forwards it to origin/main, verifies local and remote branch ancestry, then removes only clean merged worktrees. Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-toolchain.ps1 when diagnosing local CMake, CTest, CPack, clang-format, Visual Studio, MSBuild, or PATH issues; toolchain-check also enforces that visible CMake configure/build presets inherit normalized-configure-environment / normalized-build-environment so raw cmake --preset commands inherit PATH and unset Path, repository wrappers collapse parent PATH/Path variants into one child Path on Windows and PATH elsewhere for CMake/MSBuild/CTest, and reports linked-worktree vcpkg readiness; treat generated out/build/<preset> trees as disposable outputs and do not repair them by editing CMakeCache.txt, .vcxproj, .sln, or MSBuild props; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/cmake.ps1 and tools/ctest.ps1 for focused local CMake/CTest loops; tools/build.ps1 and tools/test.ps1 default to automatic CMake/CTest parallelism through official --parallel options and accept -Jobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit lower throttle; MSVC targets use bounded /MP2 plus /Zf through MK_apply_common_target_options; time-sensitive host-resource tests use CTest RUN_SERIAL rather than disabling suite-wide parallelism; reserve raw cmake --preset checks plus check-toolchain.ps1 -RequireDirectCMake for explicit direct-command requirements; use pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-format.ps1 / pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/format.ps1 for repository formatting because raw clang-format --dry-run commands require direct-clang-format-status=ready or an explicit resolved tool path, and those wrappers also check/apply tracked text normalization (LF, UTF-8 without BOM, a single final newline, and no trailing EOF blank lines); use tools/check-text-format.ps1 / tools/format-text.ps1 for text-only loops; run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate.ps1 before completion; validate.ps1 runs independent static checks through bounded parallel jobs with per-check timeouts before the sequential build, clang-tidy smoke, and CTest gate, writes complete per-script logs under ignored out/validation-logs/ while keeping console output bounded, accepts -SkipTidySmoke only when another selected lane owns full static-analysis evidence, accepts -StaticJobs <N> only when a local host needs an explicit static-check throttle, and accepts -StaticCheckTimeoutSeconds <N> only for host-specific timeout diagnostics. Hosted workflow checkouts stay SHA-pinned with permissions: contents: read and persist-credentials: false, then retry once after transient fetch/auth failure before toolchain steps; ccache stats are guarded so early checkout/toolchain failures keep the primary diagnostic visible. Hosted CI uses validate.ps1 -StaticOnly -StaticJobs 1 -StaticCheckTimeoutSeconds 120 for the dedicated static lane and validate.ps1 -SkipStaticChecks -SkipTidySmoke for the Windows MSVC lane to avoid duplicate static validation; raw Linux, sanitizer, and macOS lanes pass explicit official --parallel job counts to CMake build and CTest. Windows vcpkg package/install caches and build caches restore with actions/cache/restore and attempt fresh non-blocking actions/cache/save writes only after validation succeeds. Run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/validate-desktop-game-runtime.ps1 when the game selects desktop-game-runtime, run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 for the default sample-shell desktop-runtime-release lane, run tools/package-desktop-runtime.ps1 -GameTarget <target> for a registered desktop runtime package target with package smoke metadata and package file validation, run the explicit real-window D3D12 or Vulkan smoke command only on hosts with the matching runtime/toolchain support, or run pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/build-gui.ps1 when broader editor/Dear ImGui readiness is needed.
